# PCA GA Minutes — LLM pack (compact catalogues, one file)

Generated index for AI ingestion. The corpus lives at https://raymond-rishty.github.io/pca-ga (raw markdown at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/raymond-rishty/pca-ga/main).
This file concatenates the SMALL structured catalogues so you can load them in one fetch:
the corpus index, the RPR hub, judicial cases, constitutional inquiries, and CCB advice.
Each catalogue row deep-links to the verbatim minutes page; cite as `M<GA>GA p.<page>`.

Two catalogues are too large to inline here — fetch them directly when a question needs them:
- https://raymond-rishty.github.io/pca-ga/index/OVERTURES.md — every overture + outcome (~104k tokens)
- https://raymond-rishty.github.io/pca-ga/index/RPR-BY-PROVISION.md — RPR exceptions of substance by BCO/RAO/WCF provision (~308k tokens)



==============================================================================
# INDEX.md   (live: https://raymond-rishty.github.io/pca-ga/index/INDEX.md)
==============================================================================

# PCA General Assembly Minutes — Corpus Index

All **52 volumes** of the Presbyterian Church in America *Minutes of the General Assembly*, **1973–2025**: cleaned, OCR-corrected, structurally-formatted markdown plus structured catalogues. Everything here is plain markdown — readable, greppable, and ingestible directly into your own research or tooling.

## Catalogues

- **[Overtures](OVERTURES.md)** — every overture to every Assembly (number, source presbytery, page). *"Has the PCA considered this before?"*
- **[Judicial cases](CASES.md)** — SJC/CCB cases with parties, disposition, and the BCO provisions cited.
- **[Constitutional inquiries](INQUIRIES.md)** — questions of constitutional interpretation answered by the CCB, each with a Digest-level headnote and the verbatim record. *"What has the CCB said about this provision?"*
- **[CCB advice on overtures & amendments](CCB-OVERTURE-ADVICE.md)** — the CCB's constitutional review of proposed changes (whether an overture is *in conflict* with the Constitution).
- **[Review of Presbytery Records](RPR.md)** — exceptions of substance taken to each presbytery's minutes (the constitutional-compliance record), threaded across years with their satisfactory/unsatisfactory outcome; also **[by provision](RPR-BY-PROVISION.md)**. *"Which presbyteries were cited under this BCO provision, and was it resolved?"*
- **[Per-volume outlines](outlines/)** — a structural table of contents for each volume.
- **Full-text search:** a SQLite database (`pca_minutes.db`) indexes every page; see [../PORTABLE.md](../PORTABLE.md) for query recipes. The markdown above is generated from it.

## Volumes

| GA | Year | Minutes | Outline | Pages |
|---:|---:|---|---|---:|

# RPR.md   (live: https://raymond-rishty.github.io/pca-ga/index/RPR.md)
==============================================================================

# Review of Presbytery Records (RPR) — Exceptions of Substance

Each year the General Assembly's **Committee on Review of Presbytery Records** reviews every presbytery's minutes and flags **exceptions of substance** — apparent violations of the Constitution. This catalogue threads each exception across the years it was disputed (raised → the presbytery responds → a later GA finds the response **satisfactory** or **unsatisfactory**), drawn from the RPR appendix of every volume GA18–52 (1990–2025). The published appendix reflects the **adopted** state (floor strikes already removed); floor-action provenance is noted per row.

## Corpus

- **5941 threaded exceptions of substance** across **100 presbyteries**, GA18–52 (1990–2025), from 10624 report appearances.
- Final disposition: **3450 satisfactory (closed)**, **871 unsatisfactory (outstanding)**, **1620 raised** (most-recent year, not yet adjudicated).
- Cross-reference by provision: **[RPR exceptions by BCO/RAO/WCF provision](RPR-BY-PROVISION.md)**.

## Most-cited provisions

| Provision | Citations |
|---|---:|
| BCO 21-4 | 805 |
| RAO 16-3.e.5 | 413 |
| BCO 13-6 | 309 |
| BCO 23-1 | 308 |
| BCO 15-1 | 257 |
| BCO 18-2 | 249 |
| BCO 20-1 | 224 |
| BCO 13-11 | 206 |
| BCO 8-7 | 181 |
| BCO 13-9.b | 146 |
| BCO 19-2 | 141 |
| BCO 13-12 | 128 |
| BCO 13-7 | 117 |
| RAO 16-3.e.4 | 106 |
| BCO 15-2 | 105 |

## Citations to the Standing Judicial Commission (BCO 40-5)

Presbyteries cited to the SJC for repeated failure to submit minutes/responses or for serious non-compliance — the terminal escalation of the RPR process — with the related judicial case where one resulted.

==============================================================================
# CASES.md   (live: https://raymond-rishty.github.io/pca-ga/index/CASES.md)
==============================================================================

# Judicial Case Index

Cases decided by the Standing Judicial Commission (SJC) and its predecessor the Committee on Judicial Business (CJB), grouped by Assembly.

This index is **structure-first**: every case listed links to a full-text page re-extracted verbatim from the volume (with its opinions). After the decided cases, an Assembly may list extra rows from the underlying case table: *decided at Nth GA* — the case was only listed here (deferred to a later Assembly, or cited from an earlier one) and links to where it was actually decided; *reference / no separate decision* — a cross-reference, roll-up, or out-of-order/withdrawn matter with no published decision; *not yet re-extracted* — the volume is still pending.


## 1st General Assembly (1973)

| Case | Parties / Title | Disposition | Page |
|---|---|---|---|

| [2020-08](../cases/ga49_2022__stub_2020-08.md) | TE Shawn Gendall, TE Russell Hightower, and TE Timothy Lien Pacific Presbytery | Out of Order | [disposition](../cases/ga49_2022__stub_2020-08.md) |
| 2020-02 | Central Georgia Presbytery, Southeast Alabama Presbytery, Savannah River Presbyt | other | _no separate decision located_ · [ga49_2022 p.842](../markdown/ga49_2022.md#ga49-p825) |
| 2021-07 | RE J. Lance Acree Tennessee Valley Presbytery | dismissed | _disposed at 50th GA_ · [disposition](../cases/ga50_2023__stub_2021-07.md) |

==============================================================================
# INQUIRIES.md   (live: https://raymond-rishty.github.io/pca-ga/index/INQUIRIES.md)
==============================================================================

# Constitutional Inquiry Catalogue

Questions of *constitutional interpretation* (Westminster Standards, *Book of Church Order*, *Rules of Assembly Operations*) referred to the **Committee on Constitutional Business (CCB)** — and, before the 18th General Assembly, the Committee on Judicial Business — answered with **non-binding advice**. Grouped by Assembly.

Each entry pairs a **Digest-level headnote** (the PCA Digest's editorial summary, Part II) with the **verbatim record** sliced from the minutes; the **Minutes** column deep-links to the source page. **Subject** and **Synopsis** are distilled from the Digest's own text. The roster is drawn from the PCA Digest, Part II (1973–2018); later Assemblies are extracted directly from each volume's CCB report.

*The CCB's advice on whether proposed overtures/amendments conflict with the Constitution is catalogued separately in **[Overture & amendment advice](CCB-OVERTURE-ADVICE.md)**.*


## 3rd General Assembly (1975)  ·  `ga03_1975`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 3-89 | [Session responsibility for budgeting and the diaconate's proper role](../inquiries/ga03_1975__ci01.md) | The CCB responded that BCO 10-2 already assigns budgeting to the session, so no special instruction to sessions was necessary. | BCO 10-2 | advice given | John Holmes | [ga03_1975 p.113](../markdown/ga03_1975.md#ga03-p115) |

## 4th General Assembly (1976)  ·  `ga04_1976`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 4-71 III, A | [Session oversight of a church lacking a full session](../inquiries/ga04_1976__ci01.md) | The CCB directed Rev. Dunkerley to BCO 12-2, noting that church courts have latitude to establish rules not violating the Constitution when a congregation has only one ruling elder and no full session. | BCO 12-2 | advice given | Rev. Donald Dunkerley | [ga04_1976 p.72](../markdown/ga04_1976.md#ga04-p72) |

## 6th General Assembly (1978)  ·  `ga06_1978`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 6-111 III | [Meaning and manner of reaffirmation of faith for membership](../inquiries/ga06_1978__ci01.md) | The CCB advised that 'reaffirmation of faith' historically means answering BCO 58-5 questions 1 and 2, but a testimony of Christian experience may also satisfy it, and requesting reaffirmation of transferring members is not improper. | BCO 58-6, BCO 58-5, BCO 12-2 | advice given | Westminster Presbyterian Church, Kingsport, TN | [ga06_1978 p.112](../markdown/ga06_1978.md#ga06-p112) |

## 7th General Assembly (1979)  ·  `ga07_1979`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 7-41 III, 21 | [Ordination questions and re-ordination for ministers from other denominations](../inquiries/ga07_1979__ci01.md) | The CCB advised that BCO 13-6 requires ordination questions be put to any minister from another denomination, and presbytery may require re-ordination if deemed necessary. | BCO 13-6 | advice given |  | [ga07_1979 p.104](../markdown/ga07_1979.md#ga07-p101) |
| 7-41 III, 21 | [Ordination questions and re-ordination for ministers from other denominations](../inquiries/ga07_1979__ci02.md) | The CCB advised that BCO 13-6 requires ordination questions be put to any minister from another denomination, and presbytery may require re-ordination if deemed necessary. | BCO 13-6 | advice given |  | [ga07_1979 p.104](../markdown/ga07_1979.md#ga07-p104) |

## 9th General Assembly (1981)  ·  `ga09_1981`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 9-65 II, I, 2 | [Applicability of new internship and licensure requirement to candidates already in process](../inquiries/ga09_1981__ci01.md) | The GA adopted a procedure specifying that the new required year of licensure applies only to candidates not yet in the ordination process at the time of adoption. | BCO 19 | adopted — internship requirement applies only to new candidates not yet in process |  | [ga09_1981 p.142](../markdown/ga09_1981.md#ga09-p142) |

## 10th General Assembly (1982)  ·  `ga10_1982`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 10-23 | [General Assembly committee authority over cooperative mission agreements](../inquiries/ga10_1982__ci01.md) | The CCB advised that the Committee of Commissioners channels business to the floor rather than acting as a commission, and the full Assembly retains authority over cooperative agreements. | BCO 14-1-5, BCO 14-1-7, BCO 14-6, BCO 12-5, BCO 13-8, RAO 8-1, BCO 15-1 | advice given |  | [ga10_1982 p.60](../markdown/ga10_1982.md#ga10-p60) |
| 10-75 III, 8 | [Ordination status and restoration process after deposition from office](../inquiries/ga10_1982__ci02.md) | The CCB advised that a deposed officer has no ordination status, and restoration requires at minimum reaffirmation of vows, laying on of hands, and a proper call. | BCO 30-5, BCO 36-7, BCO 37-4, BCO 34-8, BCO 37-7 | advice given | Presbytery of Central Florida | [ga10_1982 p.99](../markdown/ga10_1982.md#ga10-p99) |
| 10-75 III, 10 | [Definition of 'voter' in congregational officer elections](../inquiries/ga10_1982__ci03.md) | A voter under BCO 24-3 is a communing member in good standing present at the meeting; only those who cast a ballot count toward the majority required for election. | BCO 24-3 | advice given | Session of Vineville Presbyterian Church | [ga10_1982 p.101](../markdown/ga10_1982.md#ga10-p101) |
| 10-77 | [Constitutionality of a non-geographical presbytery under BCO 11-4](../inquiries/ga10_1982__ci04.md) | The CCB found that the BCO does not address non-geographical presbyteries, as BCO 11-4 implies geographical bounds while BCO 13-1 does not limit 'bounds' geographically. | BCO 11-4, BCO 13-1 | advice given |  | [ga10_1982 p.107](../markdown/ga10_1982.md#ga10-p107) |

## 11th General Assembly (1983)  ·  `ga11_1983`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 11-36 III, 29 | [Presbytery authority to judge theonomy as heresy under WCF 19-4](../inquiries/ga11_1983__ci01.md) | The CCB advised that a presbytery retains the right to examine and judge a man heretical on WCF 19-4 grounds, provided the error is demonstrated by specific evidence. | WCF 19-4, BCO 18-1, BCO 18-4 | advice given | Gulf Coast Presbytery | [ga11_1983 p.96](../markdown/ga11_1983.md#ga11-p96) |
| 11-36 III, 34 | [Scope of congregational meetings beyond BCO-enumerated purposes](../inquiries/ga11_1983__ci02.md) | The CCB advised that BCO 25-2 permits congregational meetings to be called for anything deemed in the best interests of the church, beyond the stated examples. | BCO 25-2 | partial advice given | Eastern Carolina Presbytery | [ga11_1983 p.98](../markdown/ga11_1983.md#ga11-p98) |
| Recommendation 52 (SCJB Appendix) / Rec. 55 (journal) | [Presbytery approval required for amendments to pastoral call terms](../inquiries/ga11_1983__ci03.md) | Because BCO 20-1 requires presbytery approval of the original call, the CCB advised that any subsequent changes to the terms of a pastor's call also require presbytery approval. | BCO 20-1 | advice given | Presbytery of the Southwest | [ga11_1983 p.101](../markdown/ga11_1983.md#ga11-p235) |

## 12th General Assembly (1984)  ·  `ga12_1984`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 12-53 II,15 | [Prohibition of dual candidacy under BCO 18-7](../inquiries/ga12_1984__ci01.md) | BCO 18-7 permits a candidate to retain home-church membership while under another presbytery's care, but does not allow simultaneous candidacy under two presbyteries. | BCO 18-7, BCO 18-2, BCO 19-1, BCO 19-5 | advice given | Grace Presbytery | [ga12_1984 p.126](../markdown/ga12_1984.md#ga12-p126) |
| 12-53 II, 51 | [Whether a ruling elder moderator must also hold a representative commission](../inquiries/ga12_1984__ci02.md) | The CCB advised that a non-representative ruling elder may serve as presbytery moderator, but may only vote when his vote would be determinative. | BCO 10-3, BCO 13-1 | advice given | Session of the First Presbyterian Church of Stanley, North Carolina | [ga12_1984 p.137](../markdown/ga12_1984.md#ga12-p137) |
| 12-53 III | [Power of a receiving court to broaden charges in a transferred judicial case](../inquiries/ga12_1984__ci03.md) | When a judicial case is transferred, the receiving court becomes court of original jurisdiction and may add further charges pertaining to the original matter, but wholly new charges must go back to the first court. | BCO 41-3, BCO 31-2 | advice given | Judicial Commission of Gulf Coast Presbytery | [ga12_1984 p.137](../markdown/ga12_1984.md#ga12-p137) |
| 12-53 II, 54 | [Teaching elder without a call and BCO 13-5 exceptions](../inquiries/ga12_1984__ci04.md) | The CCB advised that BCO 13-5 allows a presbytery to retain a teaching elder without a call in cases it deems necessary, subject to General Assembly review. | BCO 13-5 | advice given | Presbytery of Eastern Canada | [ga12_1984 p.138](../markdown/ga12_1984.md#ga12-p138) |
| 12-53 II, 56 | [Scope and duration of BCO debate prohibitions during a complaint](../inquiries/ga12_1984__ci05.md) | BCO prohibitions on debate apply only to the specific matter complained, only until final adjudication, and to all members of the lower court — expiring upon final resolution. | BCO 39-2, BCO 42-4, BCO 43-4 | advice given | Central Carolina Presbytery | [ga12_1984 p.139](../markdown/ga12_1984.md#ga12-p139) |
| 12-53 II | [Recourse when General Assembly may have erred in a decision](../inquiries/ga12_1984__ci06.md) | The CCB advised that errors alleged during a GA meeting may be challenged under BCO 45, and subsequent assemblies may take contrary positions but cannot annul prior actions. | BCO 43-4, BCO 45 | advice given | St. Louis Presbytery | [ga12_1984 p.139](../markdown/ga12_1984.md#ga12-p139) |
| 12-53 II, 60 | [Whether congregation may set minimum voting age for elections](../inquiries/ga12_1984__ci07.md) | The BCO provides no minimum voting age for congregational meetings, except where state law imposes one for corporate votes. | BCO 6-2, BCO 6-4, BCO 24-3, BCO 25-1, BCO 25-3, BCO 25-11 | advice given | Texas Presbytery | [ga12_1984 p.140](../markdown/ga12_1984.md#ga12-p140) |
| 12-53 III, 62 | [Minimum ruling elders required to constitute a valid session](../inquiries/ga12_1984__ci08.md) | The CCB advised that one ruling elder plus a pastor constitutes a valid session, but a church without a pastor requires at least two ruling elders. | BCO 12-1 | advice given | Central Georgia Presbytery | [ga12_1984 p.141](../markdown/ga12_1984.md#ga12-p141) |
| 12-53 III,69 | [Ordination of men who believe women may serve as deacons](../inquiries/ga12_1984__ci09.md) | Believing Scripture permits women deacons, while agreeing to abide by BCO standards in practice, is not by itself sufficient grounds to deny ordination or reception in the PCA. | BCO 7-2, BCO 9-3, BCO 21-5 | advice given |  | [ga12_1984 p.142](../markdown/ga12_1984.md#ga12-p142) |
| 12-73 | [Propriety of abstractly listing disqualifying beliefs for ordination](../inquiries/ga12_1984__ci10.md) | The GA declined to determine abstractly which beliefs disqualify a man from office, holding that only proper judicial processes may make such binding determinations. | BCO 21-5 | advice given | TE Bruce W. Howes et al. | [ga12_1984 p.153](../markdown/ga12_1984.md#ga12-p153) |
| 12-83 | [Handling exceptions at ordination and distinctions between BCO and confessional exceptions](../inquiries/ga12_1984__ci11.md) | Courts must investigate stated exceptions; BCO exceptions under vow 3 are handled differently from doctrinal exceptions under vow 2, with judicial process available if warranted. | BCO 21-5, BCO 27-37 | advice given | Central Carolina Presbytery | [ga12_1984 p.165](../markdown/ga12_1984.md#ga12-p165) |

## 13th General Assembly (1985)  ·  `ga13_1985`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 13-27 | [Eligibility of expired-term trustee to fill another trustee's unexpired term](../inquiries/ga13_1985__ci01.md) | The CCB advised that BCO 14-1-12 permits such a nomination only if the agency's assembly-approved bylaws explicitly authorize it. | BCO 14-1-12 | advice given |  | [ga13_1985 p.86](../markdown/ga13_1985.md#ga13-p88) |
| 13-31 | [Trustee with expired term filling another's unexpired vacancy](../inquiries/ga13_1985__ci02.md) | The CCB reaffirmed that BCO 14-1-12 permits an expired-term trustee to fill an unexpired vacancy only if agency bylaws approved by the Assembly specifically allow it. | BCO 14-1-12 | advice given |  | [ga13_1985 p.91](../markdown/ga13_1985.md#ga13-p93) |
| 13-45 III, 47 | [Administrative Committee acting as an unlimited commission under Standing Rule 16](../inquiries/ga13_1985__ci03.md) | The CCB advised that Standing Rule 16 effectively created an unlimited commission contrary to BCO 15-1, which restricts commissions to specifically defined matters. | BCO 15-1, BCO 19-2, BCO 21-4, WCF XXVII:4 | advice given |  | [ga13_1985 p.108](../markdown/ga13_1985.md#ga13-p110) |
| 13-45 III,61 | [Proper limits on presbyterial use of the extraordinary clause](../inquiries/ga13_1985__ci04.md) | The GA counseled that use of BCO 21-4's extraordinary clause must be limited to truly extraordinary circumstances or proven extraordinary gifts, warning against increasing laxity in application. | BCO 21-4 | advice given | Louisiana Presbytery | [ga13_1985 p.110](../markdown/ga13_1985.md#ga13-p112) |
| 13-45 III, 65 | [Presbytery authority to conduct regular visitation of congregations](../inquiries/ga13_1985__ci05.md) | The CCB advised that BCO 13-9 grants presbytery the prerogative to initiate regular visitation of its churches for their spiritual welfare. | BCO 13-9 | advice given | Missouri Presbytery | [ga13_1985 p.111](../markdown/ga13_1985.md#ga13-p113) |
| CI #1 | [Meaning of willful neglect and gross crime under BCO 46-5 and 30-4](../inquiries/ga13_1985__ci06.md) | BCO 46-5 requires due process under BCO 27-5 for incorrigible members, and 'gross crime or heresy' is to be understood in its biblical and moral sense as determined by the church court. | BCO 46-5, BCO 27-5, BCO 33-3, BCO 30-4 | advice given | Session of the Town North Presbyterian Church of Richardson, Texas | [ga13_1985 p.107](../markdown/ga13_1985.md#ga13-p241) |
| CI #2 | [Proper limits on presbyterial use of the extraordinary clause](../inquiries/ga13_1985__ci07.md) | The GA counseled that use of BCO 21-4's extraordinary clause must be limited to truly extraordinary circumstances or proven extraordinary gifts, warning against increasing laxity in application. | BCO 21-4 | advice given | Louisiana Presbytery | [ga13_1985 p.110](../markdown/ga13_1985.md#ga13-p241) |
| CI #7 | [Voting rights of an associate pastor when not moderating the session](../inquiries/ga13_1985__ci08.md) | The CCB advised that an associate pastor, as a session member, retains full voting rights even when not serving as moderator. | BCO 4-3, BCO 12-1, BCO 22-2 | advice given | Session of Evangelical Presbyterian Church of Levittown, PA | [ga13_1985 p.109](../markdown/ga13_1985.md#ga13-p243) |
| CI #8 | [Voting rights of Elders and Deacons Emeriti after BCO 24-9 amendment](../inquiries/ga13_1985__ci09.md) | The CCB noted that the Twelfth GA amended BCO 24-9 to prohibit Emeriti from voting, while a grandfather clause preserved prior voting privileges for those already holding them. | BCO 24-9 | advice given | Session of the Antioch Presbyterian Church of Woodruff, SC | [ga13_1985 p.109](../markdown/ga13_1985.md#ga13-p243) |
| CI #13 | [Notice of complaint sufficiency and timing of suspension motion under BCO 43-4](../inquiries/ga13_1985__ci10.md) | Northeast Presbytery asked whether a written statement of intent to complain constitutes adequate notice to suspend a court's action and when a suspension motion is procedurally in order. | BCO 43-4, BCO 43-2 | advice given | Northeast Presbytery | [ga13_1985 p.109](../markdown/ga13_1985.md#ga13-p247) |
| CI #15 | [Scope of presbytery commissions under BCO 15-2](../inquiries/ga13_1985__ci11.md) | The CCB advised that BCO 15-2's list of matters assignable to commissions is typical but not exhaustive, with other matters assignable subject to review and control. | BCO 15-2 | advice given | Presbytery of Western Carolinas | [ga13_1985 p.109](../markdown/ga13_1985.md#ga13-p247) |
| CI #16 | [Applicability of extraordinary clause to ministers from other denominations](../inquiries/ga13_1985__ci12.md) | The CCB advised that neither BCO 13-6 nor BCO 21-4 contains an extraordinary clause applicable to ministers transferring from other denominations. | BCO 13-6, BCO 21-4 | advice given | Presbytery of the Western Carolinas | [ga13_1985 p.109](../markdown/ga13_1985.md#ga13-p247) |

## 14th General Assembly (1986)  ·  `ga14_1986`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 14-42 | [Definition of 'appearance' before an adjudicating commission under BCO 42-11](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci01.md) | The CCB defined 'appearance' as the personal presence of a party or counsel before the adjudicating body after that body has given reasonable notice. | BCO 42-11 | advice given | Commission to adjudicate Case #4 | [ga14_1986 p.112](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p112) |
| 14-44 | [Constituting a judicial commission under BCO 15-2](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci02.md) | A judicial commission requires both appointment under BCO 15-2 with instructions to adjudicate and prior approval of charges for process under BCO 32-3. | BCO 15-2, BCO 32-3 | advice given | Commission to adjudicate Case #5 | [ga14_1986 p.120](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p120) |
| 14-52 45 | [Teaching elder voting rights and congregational approval of pastoral salary changes](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci03.md) | The CCB advised that a teaching elder votes as a full session member, and that any change to pastoral salary terms requires congregational approval as part of the call. | BCO 4-3, BCO 12-1, BCO 22-2, BCO 20-6, BCO 20-1 | advice given | Faith Presbyterian Church | [ga14_1986 p.128](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p124) |
| 14-52 10 | [Admitting non-members in good standing to the Lord's Table](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci04.md) | The GA left to the spiritual discernment of the session whether to permit a person who is not a member in good standing of an evangelical church to participate in the Lord's Supper. | BCO 58-4 | left to spiritual discernment of Session | Westminster Presbyterian Church | [ga14_1986 p.125](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p125) |
| 14-52 13 | [Liberty granted by exceptions versus right to teach contrary to standards](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci05.md) | Ordination with exceptions grants personal liberty to hold minority views but does not warrant public teaching or preaching of those views in ways that disturb the church's peace and purity. | BCO 21-5 | advice given |  | [ga14_1986 p.125](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p125) |
| 14-52 48 | [Legality of dividing presbytery into sections to hear candidate sermons](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci06.md) | The full presbytery must conduct all trial parts; dividing into sections to hear candidate sermons for ordination is impermissible except as a commission and should be treated as exceptional. | BCO 21-4 | advice given | Southeast Alabama Presbytery | [ga14_1986 p.129](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p127) |
| 14-52 45 | [Teaching elder voting rights and congregational approval of pastoral salary changes](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci07.md) | The CCB advised that a teaching elder votes as a full session member, and that any change to pastoral salary terms requires congregational approval as part of the call. | BCO 4-3, BCO 12-1, BCO 22-2, BCO 20-6, BCO 20-1 | advice given | Faith Presbyterian Church | [ga14_1986 p.128](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p128) |
| CI #3 | [Grounds and procedures for removing members from PCA church rolls](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci08.md) | Declining to address case-specific questions, the CCB outlined the three BCO provisions by which members may be removed from church rolls: transfer, discipline, or relocation. | BCO 38-3, BCO 27-5, BCO 46-2, BCO 46-3, BCO 46-4 | declined to answer specific questions; general advice given | Presbytery of North Texas | [ga14_1986 p.125](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p332) |
| CI #4 | [Whether deletion from the roll under BCO 46-5 constitutes excommunication](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci09.md) | The CCB answered yes — 'deletion' under BCO 46-5 is excommunication in effect, and the due-process procedures of BCO 27-5 must therefore be followed. | BCO 46-5, BCO 27-5 | advice given | Trinity Presbyterian Church | [ga14_1986 p.126](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p332) |
| CI #6 | [Procedure for removing members who relocate without affiliating elsewhere](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci10.md) | BCO 46-2 governs removal of members who move away and fail to affiliate within a year; BCO 46-5's formal discipline applies only to willful neglect, not mere relocation. | BCO 46-2, BCO 46-5, BCO 27-5 | advice given | Stoney Point Reformed Presbyterian Church, Richmond, VA | [ga14_1986 p.126](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p333) |
| CI #8 | [Teaching elder voting rights and congregational approval of pastoral salary changes](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci11.md) | The CCB advised that a teaching elder votes as a full session member, and that any change to pastoral salary terms requires congregational approval as part of the call. | BCO 4-3, BCO 12-1, BCO 22-2, BCO 20-6, BCO 20-1, BCO 24-6, BCO 13-9, BCO 38-2, WCF XXVII, WCF XXIX, BCO 46-5, BCO 27-5 | advice given | Faith Presbyterian Church | [ga14_1986 p.128](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p333) |
| CI #9 | [Rotating sessions, ballot control, and officer election procedures](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci12.md) | The CCB answered five questions on officer elections: the session must present all eligible men, the congregation determines the number elected, and no constitutional limit restricts election frequency beyond a 30-day notice. | BCO 24-1 | advice given | Covenant Presbyterian Church | [ga14_1986 p.126](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p334) |
| CI #10 | [Effect of GA refusal to accept a judicial reference on lower-court proceedings](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci13.md) | The committee's recommendation and the Assembly's determination refusing a judicial reference apply to the entire judicial process in PCA courts, clarifying that no parties were left without recourse. | BCO 41-5 | advice given | Presbytery of Eastern Canada | [ga14_1986 p.126](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p334) |
| CI #11 | [Written sermon requirement for licensure under BCO 19-2](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci14.md) | Both a written and oral sermon are required for licensure; the written sermon must be available to the full presbytery, while the oral sermon may be presented before the presbytery or its committee. | BCO 19-2 | advice given | Presbytery of the Ascension | [ga14_1986 p.126](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p335) |
| CI #12 | [Effect of appeal on suspension of disciplinary censure during review](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci15.md) | The CCB advised that a judgment of divestiture is suspended during appeal to the General Assembly unless the lower court separately places the censure into immediate effect. | BCO 30-1, BCO 30-5, BCO 34-10, BCO 42-6 | advice given | Great Lakes Presbytery | [ga14_1986 p.126](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p335) |
| CI #13 | [Call of assistant pastor from another denomination without presbyterial examination](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci16.md) | A session may not call an ordained assistant pastor from another denomination without presbytery approval and examination, as all teaching elders must be members of the serving presbytery. | BCO 12-4, BCO 20-1, BCO 21-1, BCO 21-5, BCO 21-6, BCO 21-7, BCO 21-9, BCO 22-3, BCO 22-4, BCO 23-1 | advice given | Faith Presbyterian Church | [ga14_1986 p.128](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p337) |
| CI #14 | [Judicial power of visitation commissions absent formal charges](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci17.md) | A presbytery commission visiting a church affected with disorder may not be granted judicial power when no charges exist and no case is pending. | BCO 15-2, BCO 31-2 | advice given | Palmetto Presbytery | [ga14_1986 p.128](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p338) |
| CI #15 | [Whether congregation may dissolve the session by vote](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci18.md) | A congregation cannot dissolve the session; it may by majority vote request individual officer removals under BCO 24-6, but the final decision rests with the session, and presbytery may assume jurisdiction under BCO 13-9. | BCO 24-6, BCO 13-9 | advice given | Covenant Presbyterian Church | [ga14_1986 p.128](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p338) |
| CI #17 | [Mandatory review of all previously unreviewed session minutes](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci19.md) | The CCB advised that it is mandatory for a presbytery to review all previously unreviewed session records, not merely resume with current ones. | BCO 13-9, BCO 40-1, BCO 40-2, BCO 40-3, BCO 40-4 | advice given | Ascension Presbytery | [ga14_1986 p.128](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p338) |
| CI #18 | [Whether deletion from the roll under BCO 46-5 constitutes excommunication](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci20.md) | The CCB answered yes — 'deletion' under BCO 46-5 is excommunication in effect, and the due-process procedures of BCO 27-5 must therefore be followed. | BCO 46-5, BCO 27-5 | advice given | Trinity Presbyterian Church | [ga14_1986 p.126](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p339) |
| CI #19 | [Legality of dividing presbytery into sections to hear candidate sermons](../inquiries/ga14_1986__ci21.md) | The full presbytery must conduct all trial parts; dividing into sections to hear candidate sermons for ordination is impermissible except as a commission and should be treated as exceptional. | BCO 21-4 | advice given | Southeast Alabama Presbytery | [ga14_1986 p.129](../markdown/ga14_1986.md#ga14-p339) |

## 15th General Assembly (1987)  ·  `ga15_1987`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 15-62 1 | [Informal versus formal admonition and due-process requirements under BCO 27-5](../inquiries/ga15_1987__ci01.md) | The CCB distinguished informal admonition (ordinary relational correction) from formal judicial admonition, holding that the paragraph in question did not require BCO 27-5 due process. | BCO 27-5, BCO 30-2, BCO 36-3, BCO 14-71 | advice given |  | [ga15_1987 p.128](../markdown/ga15_1987.md#ga15-p128) |
| 15-83 III, 8 | [Erasure of a teaching elder seeking transfer to independent church](../inquiries/ga15_1987__ci02.md) | A presbytery wishing to release a minister to an independent church should handle it by erasure under BCO 38-3, which does not affect his ordination unless the church is heretical. | BCO 38-3, BCO 13-9 | advice given | Central Carolina Presbytery | [ga15_1987 p.170](../markdown/ga15_1987.md#ga15-p170) |
| 15-83 III, 9 | [Deacons delegating the treasurer role to a non-deacon congregation member](../inquiries/ga15_1987__ci03.md) | The CCB advised that delegating the treasurer's responsibilities to a non-deacon member of the congregation does not conflict with BCO 9-4. | BCO 9-4 | advice given | All Saints Reformed Presbyterian Church, Richmond, VA | [ga15_1987 p.170](../markdown/ga15_1987.md#ga15-p170) |
| 15-83 III, 10 | [Emeritus officers voting in higher courts and local bodies](../inquiries/ga15_1987__ci04.md) | The CCB affirmed that an Elder Emeritus may not vote in the local session but may vote as a delegate to Presbytery if appointed to serve there. | BCO 24-9 | advice given | Town North Presbyterian Church, Richardson, TX | [ga15_1987 p.170](../markdown/ga15_1987.md#ga15-p170) |
| 15-83 III, 11 | [Stated supply's role as session moderator and congregational meeting chair](../inquiries/ga15_1987__ci05.md) | The CCB advised that a stated supply becomes moderator only by session invitation or presbytery appointment, and a session member, not the stated supply, should open congregational meetings. | BCO 12-3, BCO 24-2 | advice given | Central Carolina Presbytery | [ga15_1987 p.171](../markdown/ga15_1987.md#ga15-p171) |
| 15-83 III, 13 | [Role of a presbytery evangelist in church discipline and membership actions](../inquiries/ga15_1987__ci06.md) | The CCB advised that an evangelist may grant membership transfers but may not remove names from rolls or impose discipline, as those are acts reserved to church courts. | BCO 8-6, BCO 3-2, BCO 5-5 | advice given | Calvin Presbyterian Church, Phoenix, AZ | [ga15_1987 p.172](../markdown/ga15_1987.md#ga15-p174) |
| 15-83 III,21 | [Jurisdiction over candidates and licentiates moving to a new field](../inquiries/ga15_1987__ci07.md) | The BCO does not specify which presbytery holds jurisdiction over candidates or licentiates moving to a new field, leaving each case to the receiving presbytery's discretion. | BCO 21-1, BCO 21-2 | advice given | Ascension Presbytery | [ga15_1987 p.176](../markdown/ga15_1987.md#ga15-p176) |

## 16th General Assembly (1988)  ·  `ga16_1988`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 16-67 V,6 | [Dividing presbytery into parts to hear ordination candidate sermons](../inquiries/ga16_1988__ci01.md) | BCO 21-4 does not permit presbyteries to divide simply into committees to hear ordination sermons; division into commissions is not prohibited but should be exceptional, not regular practice. | BCO 21-4 | advice given |  | [ga16_1988 p.164](../markdown/ga16_1988.md#ga16-p164) |
| 16-77 III,9 | [Scope of the extraordinary clause for ordination candidates](../inquiries/ga16_1988__ci02.md) | The CCB advised that under BCO 18-2 the extraordinary clause excuses only the six-month membership period, not the session endorsement, the two-month filing deadline, or the one-year internship sequence. | BCO 18-2, BCO 19-7, BCO 21-4 | advice given | Central Carolina Presbytery | [ga16_1988 p.172](../markdown/ga16_1988.md#ga16-p172) |
| 16-77 III,9 | [Extraordinary clause exemptions from licensure examinations](../inquiries/ga16_1988__ci03.md) | In extraordinary cases a presbytery may exempt a candidate from any of the prescribed parts of the licensure examination under BCO 19-2, though the clause must not be used as a subterfuge to avoid historic training requirements. | BCO 19-2, BCO 21-4 | advice given |  | [ga16_1988 p.172](../markdown/ga16_1988.md#ga16-p172) |
| 16-77 III,9 | [Pre-care service counting toward internship year under BCO 19-7](../inquiries/ga16_1988__ci04.md) | A presbytery may designate service rendered before a candidate came under care or was licensed—such as service as a ruling elder supply—as fulfilling the internship requirement under BCO 19-16. | BCO 19-7, BCO 19-16 | advice given |  | [ga16_1988 p.172](../markdown/ga16_1988.md#ga16-p172) |
| 16-77 III,9 | [Scope of extraordinary clause relative to educational prerequisites](../inquiries/ga16_1988__ci05.md) | BCO 21-4's extraordinary clause applies only to listed trial parts for ordination, not to prerequisite requirements such as college diploma, seminary diploma, and internship completion. | BCO 21-4 | advice given |  | [ga16_1988 p.172](../markdown/ga16_1988.md#ga16-p172) |
| 16-77 III, 26 | [Session limiting eligible nominees on officer election ballot](../inquiries/ga16_1988__ci06.md) | A session may not withhold qualified nominees from the congregation's ballot to limit board size; BCO 24-1 requires all eligible men to be reported to the congregation. | BCO 24-1 | advice given | Covenant Presbyterian Church, Palm Bay, Florida | [ga16_1988 p.176](../markdown/ga16_1988.md#ga16-p176) |
| 16-77 III,27 | [Dissolution of pastoral charge outside presbytery's jurisdiction](../inquiries/ga16_1988__ci07.md) | BCO 23-1 does not apply to non-PCA churches outside presbytery jurisdiction; a congregational vote cannot substitute for that chapter's dissolution process. | BCO 23-1, BCO 4-3 | advice given | Presbytery of the Ascension | [ga16_1988 p.177](../markdown/ga16_1988.md#ga16-p177) |
| 16-77 III | [Membership status and session authority upon dissolution of a congregation](../inquiries/ga16_1988__ci08.md) | Upon dissolution of a congregation, the session terminates, membership in the visible church is dissolved for those not transferred, and Presbytery may not unilaterally assign members to other churches. | BCO 46-5, BCO 25-12, BCO 46-8, BCO 46-3 | advice given | Presbytery of the Ascension | [ga16_1988 p.177](../markdown/ga16_1988.md#ga16-p177) |
| 16-77 III | [Whether a resignation letter constitutes willful neglect justifying roll erasure](../inquiries/ga16_1988__ci09.md) | The CCB held that 'deletion' under BCO 46-5 is equivalent to excommunication and referred the question of easier non-judicial removal to the 17th GA for potential BCO amendment. | BCO 46-5, BCO 46, BCO 46-3, BCO 27-5 | advice given | Stony Point Reformed Presbyterian Church | [ga16_1988 p.178](../markdown/ga16_1988.md#ga16-p178) |
| 16-77 III, 30 | [Session authority over officer nominees and inactive elders under rotation](../inquiries/ga16_1988__ci10.md) | A session must present all qualified nominees for election and may not use BCO 24-6 to exclude inactive officers; presbytery proceedings are not automatically required for long-inactive elders. | BCO 24-6, BCO 24-7, BCO 24-1, BCO 13-2, BCO 38-2, BCO 34-10 | advice given | Town North Presbyterian Church, Richardson, TX | [ga16_1988 p.178](../markdown/ga16_1988.md#ga16-p178) |

## 17th General Assembly (1989)  ·  `ga17_1989`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 17-82 III | [Certification of presbytery dismissal by committee chairman's letter](../inquiries/ga17_1989__ci01.md) | A letter from a presbytery committee chairman is insufficient evidence of dismissal; BCO 46-6 requires a certificate bearing the stated clerk's signature per BCO 10-4. | BCO 46-6, BCO 10-4 | advice given | Review and Control Committee | [ga17_1989 p.151](../markdown/ga17_1989.md#ga17-p151) |
| 17-82 III, 8 | [Applicability of BCO 10-3 sermon and moderator-absence procedures to presbyteries](../inquiries/ga17_1989__ci02.md) | The CCB advised that BCO 10-3 is ambiguous on moderator absence, leaving each presbytery to interpret it, and ruled that telephone conference meetings are impermissible. | BCO 10-3, BCO 13-4 | advice given | Review and Control Committee | [ga17_1989 p.151](../markdown/ga17_1989.md#ga17-p151) |
| 17-82 III,17 | [Implementation of 1988 BCO amendments on Assistant Pastors](../inquiries/ga17_1989__ci03.md) | Tennessee Valley Presbytery asked whether the new BCO 22-3/22-4 Assistant Pastor provisions applied retroactively, to all full-time ministers, and whether a lesser examination was appropriate. | BCO 13-2, BCO 13-3, BCO 20-1, BCO 21-1, BCO 22-3, BCO 22-4 | advice given | Tennessee Valley Presbytery | [ga17_1989 p.156](../markdown/ga17_1989.md#ga17-p156) |
| 17-82 III, 20 | [Re-examination and divestiture of officers in rotational systems](../inquiries/ga17_1989__ci05.md) | The CCB advised that the BCO provides no procedure for re-examining rotated-off officers' qualifications, but divestiture may only occur under BCO 24-6, 38-2, or 34-10. | BCO 24-6, BCO 24-1, BCO 38-2, BCO 34-10 | advice given | Town North Presbyterian Church, Richardson, Texas | [ga17_1989 p.157](../markdown/ga17_1989.md#ga17-p157) |
| 17-82 III,21 | [Congregational and presbyterial approval of call term changes](../inquiries/ga17_1989__ci06.md) | Changes to a pastoral call's terms require both congregational approval under BCO 20-6 and presbytery approval, consistent with the 11th General Assembly's prior ruling on BCO 20-1. | BCO 20-1, BCO 20-6 | advice given | Northeast Presbytery | [ga17_1989 p.158](../markdown/ga17_1989.md#ga17-p158) |
| 17-82 III, 22 | [Presbytery jurisdiction and restoration path for a deposed and repentant minister](../inquiries/ga17_1989__ci07.md) | The CCB advised that restoration of a deposed minister follows BCO 37-4 and 37-7 exclusively, and BCO 18–21 ordination procedures do not apply. | BCO 37-4, BCO 37-7, BCO 18, BCO 34-8, BCO 36-7 | advice given | Presbytery of the Ascension | [ga17_1989 p.158](../markdown/ga17_1989.md#ga17-p158) |
| 17-82 III,39 | [Intern preaching without licensure and definition of regular basis](../inquiries/ga17_1989__ci08.md) | An intern may complete his internship and test his preaching gift without being licensed, and each presbytery may determine what constitutes preaching on a regular basis. | BCO 19-7, BCO 19-8 | advice given | Ascension Presbytery | [ga17_1989 p.168](../markdown/ga17_1989.md#ga17-p168) |

## 18th General Assembly (1990)  ·  `ga18_1990`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 18-19 under Communication 2 | [Proper use of memorials and finality of adjudicated GA decisions](../inquiries/ga18_1990__ci01.md) | A memorial under BCO 40-5 is the proper recourse for alleging GA error after adjournment; the Assembly will not reopen a finally adjudicated case except to correct a manifest error. | BCO 40-5, BCO 45, BCO 42-6, BCO 43-4, WCF XXIII-3 | advice given |  | [ga18_1990 p.79](../markdown/ga18_1990.md#ga18-p79) |

## 19th General Assembly (1991)  ·  `ga19_1991`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 19-48 | [Meaning of 'stay of proceedings' under BCO 40-5](../inquiries/ga19_1991__ci01.md) | Although 'stay of further proceedings' technically means holding a matter in abeyance, the CCB found that the 17th and 18th General Assemblies clearly intended the stay to finally terminate the matter. | BCO 40-5 | advice given |  | [ga19_1991 p.100](../markdown/ga19_1991.md#ga19-p100) |
| 19-48 | [Voluntary resignation from membership and application of BCO 46-5](../inquiries/ga19_1991__ci02.md) | The GA clarified that BCO 46-5 had been variously interpreted regarding voluntary resignation, resolving that a simple resignation does not automatically trigger the willful-neglect excommunication process. | BCO 46-5, BCO 12-5, BCO 27-5 | advice given |  | [ga19_1991 p.102](../markdown/ga19_1991.md#ga19-p102) |
| 19-48 | [Voluntary association principle applied to individual church membership](../inquiries/ga19_1991__ci03.md) | The CCB affirmed that the principle of voluntary association governing churches also applies to individuals joining a local PCA congregation. | BCO 25-11, BCO 46-5 | advice given |  | [ga19_1991 p.104](../markdown/ga19_1991.md#ga19-p104) |

## 20th General Assembly (1992)  ·  `ga20_1992`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 20-62 | [Minister from another denomination serving as stated supply](../inquiries/ga20_1992__ci01.md) | A minister from another denomination licensed by a PCA presbytery may serve as stated supply but may not administer sacraments unless his denomination has fraternal relations with the PCA or his sacramental views are consistent with PCA standards. | BCO 19-1, BCO 22-5, BCO 22-6 | advice given | James River Presbytery | [ga20_1992 p.127](../markdown/ga20_1992.md#ga20-p127) |
| 20-67 1 | [SJC authority to issue decisions contradicting prior General Assembly actions](../inquiries/ga20_1992__ci02.md) | The CCB advised that the SJC may adjudicate cases differently from prior Assembly actions, and that SJC decisions are not debatable on the Assembly floor. | WCF 31-3, BCO 15-15 | advice given | Dr. Morton H. Smith | [ga20_1992 p.137](../markdown/ga20_1992.md#ga20-p137) |
| 20-67 2 | [Review and control action while a judicial case is pending on appeal](../inquiries/ga20_1992__ci03.md) | A higher court may note an exception through review and control but may take no action on it while judicial proceedings are pending; the effect of any unconstitutional act is suspended upon appeal. | BCO 40-3, BCO 42-6, BCO 43-4 | advice given | TE Roy Taylor | [ga20_1992 p.138](../markdown/ga20_1992.md#ga20-p138) |
| 20-75 | [Right to complain against a court's reception of an intemperate protest](../inquiries/ga20_1992__ci04.md) | A presbyter may complain against a court's decision to receive a protest alleged to contain intemperate or disrespectful language, since that reception is itself a court action under BCO 43-1. | BCO 43-1, BCO 45-5 | advice given |  | [ga20_1992 p.267](../markdown/ga20_1992.md#ga20-p267) |

## 21st General Assembly (1993)  ·  `ga21_1993`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 21-56 III | [Permissible scope of communion practice under BCO 58-4](../inquiries/ga21_1993__ci01.md) | The GA denied closed communion (members of the local congregation only) as incompatible with BCO 58-4, affirming open or close communion while requiring baptism as a prerequisite to the Table. | BCO 58-4, 1 Corinthians 10:21, 1 Corinthians 10:16-17, Hebrews 10:25, Ephesians 4:11-12, Hebrews 13:17, 1 Thessalonians 5:12-13, Exodus 32:32-33, Daniel 12:1, Luke 10:20, Philippians 4:3, Revelation 13:8, Revelation 20:12, Revelation 20:15, Acts 2:41, Acts 2:47, Acts 6:7, 1 Timothy 5:9, 1 Corinthians 5:2, 1 Corinthians 14:23, 2 Corinthians 2:6-7, Hebrews 13:7, Hebrews 13:17-18, 1 Thessalonians 5:11-14, 1 Peter 5:2, Acts 20:28, Acts 18:27, Romans 16:1-2, 2 Corinthians 3:1, 2 Corinthians 8:23-24, 1 Corinthians 16:3, 2 Corinthians 8:16-24 | overture denied; advice given | Third Reformed Presbyterian Church / Philadelphia Presbytery | [ga21_1993 p.141](../markdown/ga21_1993.md#ga21-p141) |

## 22nd General Assembly (1994)  ·  `ga22_1994`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 22-13 2 | [Sequence of placing a call versus examining a candidate under BCO 21-1](../inquiries/ga22_1994__ci01.md) | The CCB found ambiguity in BCO 21-1 regarding whether a call must be placed before or after examination, and advised that existing presbytery practice of each presbytery determining its own sequence should prevail until the GA acts. | BCO 21-1 | advice given — ambiguity exists; current practice (each presbytery determines sequence) should prevail |  | [ga22_1994 p.70](../markdown/ga22_1994.md#ga22-p70) |
| 22-13 3.1 | [Constitutionality of GA directive to omit financial amounts from presbytery minutes](../inquiries/ga22_1994__ci02.md) | The CCB advised that directing presbyteries to omit financial amounts from call-change records was constitutional as an exercise of GA oversight, but any actual editing of an already-adopted minute record was unconstitutional. | BCO 13-10, RAO 14-10 d.3 | advice given — action partially constitutional, editing of record unconstitutional |  | [ga22_1994 p.70](../markdown/ga22_1994.md#ga22-p70) |
| 22-13 4 | [Referent of 'it' in BCO 15-1 regarding commission records](../inquiries/ga22_1994__ci03.md) | The CCB concluded that 'it' in BCO 15-1 line 6 refers to the concluding actions of the commission, not the full record of proceedings, consistent with BCO 15-3's requirement that the commission's judgment be entered as the presbytery's action. | BCO 15-1, BCO 15-3 | advice given — 'it' refers to concluding actions of the commission |  | [ga22_1994 p.71](../markdown/ga22_1994.md#ga22-p71) |
| 22-13 5 | [Parliamentary procedure for adopting committee reports at General Assembly](../inquiries/ga22_1994__ci04.md) | RAO 17-3 requires separate adoption of each committee resolution, superseding Robert's Rules' normal practice of a single motion to adopt the full report. | RAO 17-3, RAO 17-3-d, RAO 12-2 | advice given — adopted |  | [ga22_1994 p.72](../markdown/ga22_1994.md#ga22-p72) |

## 23rd General Assembly (1995)  ·  `ga23_1995`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 23-15 II, Reference No. 1 | [Congregational vote required on pastor's financial severance](../inquiries/ga23_1995__ci01.md) | BCO 23-1 requires a congregational vote on a financial severance package when a pastor resigns his call without another call. | BCO 23-1 | advice given — yes, congregation must vote on financial severance | Central Georgia Presbytery | [ga23_1995 p.56](../markdown/ga23_1995.md#ga23-p56) |
| 23-15 II | [Judicial process against a member who resigns from the roll](../inquiries/ga23_1995__ci02.md) | The CCB declined to advise, finding the reference improper, but noted that GA Judicial Case 93-3 does not bar full formal discipline of a member who tenders a letter of resignation. | BCO 46-5, BCO 14-7 | declined to advise; reference returned as improper | Ascension Presbytery | [ga23_1995 p.56](../markdown/ga23_1995.md#ga23-p56) |
| 23-43 31.d | [Ordination of candidate with exceptions to six-day creation and male-only office](../inquiries/ga23_1995__ci03.md) | New River Presbytery's response to prior exceptions was found satisfactory by the GA, though the Review Committee urged the presbytery not to approve future candidates whose exceptions deny fundamentals such as six-day creation or male-only ordination. | WCF 4-1, LC 15, BCO 7-2 | response approved as satisfactory | New River Presbytery | [ga23_1995 p.197](../markdown/ga23_1995.md#ga23-p197) |

## 24th General Assembly (1996)  ·  `ga24_1996`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 24-23 ref. #2 | [Re-examination of previously ordained officer nominees by session](../inquiries/ga24_1996__ci02.md) | The CCB advised that all nominees for ruling elder or deacon, including previously ordained individuals, must be re-examined by the session, which retains the right to find them deficient. | BCO 24-1, BCO 24-6, BCO 13-6, BCO 21-4 | advice given — re-examination required; session may find nominee deficient | Southeast Alabama Presbytery | [ga24_1996 p.160](../markdown/ga24_1996.md#ga24-p160) |
| 24-23 ref. #3 | [Six-month PCA membership requirement for ministerial candidacy from non-PCA church](../inquiries/ga24_1996__ci03.md) | The CCB advised that BCO 18-2 probably requires PCA congregation membership for candidacy, but acknowledged varying presbytery practice and recommended an overture to clarify whether endorsement from a non-PCA ruling body is permissible. | BCO 18-2, BCO 18-1 | advice given — BCO 18-2 probably requires PCA membership; overture recommended |  | [ga24_1996 p.160](../markdown/ga24_1996.md#ga24-p160) |

## 25th General Assembly (1997)  ·  `ga25_1997`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 25-17 2 | [Proposed BCO amendment permitting minimum voting age for members](../inquiries/ga25_1997__ci01.md) | A proposed amendment to BCO 11-2 that would have set a minimum voting age for communicant members was defeated by the presbyteries. | BCO 11-2 | proposed amendments defeated by presbyteries |  | [ga25_1997 p.114](../markdown/ga25_1997.md#ga25-p114) |
| 25-22 II, 1 | [Declaring exceptions for non-literal six-day creationist views](../inquiries/ga25_1997__ci02.md) | The CCB declined to act, ruling that defining what constitutes an exception to the standards is beyond its scope; presbyteries wishing to set such a norm should overture the GA. | BCO 41-5, RAO 7-2(2), BCO 41-1 | declined to take action — reference not proper; overture recommended for proper procedure | New River Presbytery | [ga25_1997 p.128](../markdown/ga25_1997.md#ga25-p128) |
| 25-22 V | [Whether a church court may accept a member's resignation](../inquiries/ga25_1997__ci04.md) | The CCB advised there is no constitutional bar preventing a court from accepting a member's resignation and removing that person's name from the roll. | BCO 46-5, BCO 38-4 | advice given — no constitutional bar to accepting resignation |  | [ga25_1997 p.131](../markdown/ga25_1997.md#ga25-p131) |
| 25-22 2nd Supplemental Report, I, 1 | [SJC authority to adjudicate issues not referred to it](../inquiries/ga25_1997__ci05.md) | The CCB opined that the Standing Judicial Commission may not adjudicate any issue or matter not referred to it, as initiating new issues would improperly make the SJC a court of original jurisdiction. | BCO 15-1 | advice given — SJC cannot adjudicate issues not referred to it |  | [ga25_1997 p.134](../markdown/ga25_1997.md#ga25-p134) |
| 25-22 ref. #2 | [Setting a nomination deadline before the one-month officer election notice](../inquiries/ga25_1997__ci06.md) | A session may set a nomination deadline before the one-month congregational meeting notice expires, but all six BCO 24-1 requirements for officer election must be met. | BCO 24-1, BCO 41-1, BCO 41-2, BCO 41-3, BCO 41-5 | advice given — session may set nomination deadline before one-month meeting notice; all six required elements must be included | Grace Presbytery | [ga25_1997 p.136](../markdown/ga25_1997.md#ga25-p136) |

## 26th General Assembly (1998)  ·  `ga26_1998`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 26-68 reference #R-1 | [Whether presbytery may investigate before proceeding to trial](../inquiries/ga26_1998__ci01.md) | BCO 31-2 permits a presbytery to investigate and determine strong presumption of guilt before adjudication; the CCB recommended amendments to BCO 31, 32, and 34 to clarify. | BCO 31-2, BCO 34-2, BCO 31, BCO 32, BCO 34 | advice given — BCO 31-2 permits investigation before adjudication; amendments recommended |  | [ga26_1998 p.228](../markdown/ga26_1998.md#ga26-p228) |
| 26-68 1st Supplemental Report, III | [Dissolution of pastoral call and supermajority vote requirements](../inquiries/ga26_1998__ci02.md) | BCO 23-1 refers back to BCO 20 for meeting procedure for pastoral dissolution; a 2/3 vote requirement must be explicitly adopted by the congregation to apply to dissolution. | BCO 23-1, BCO 20, BCO 20-4, BCO 3-1 | advice given — BCO 23-1 refers back to BCO 20 for meeting and conduct; 2/3 vote for dissolution requires explicit congregational adoption | Korean Capital Presbytery | [ga26_1998 p.231](../markdown/ga26_1998.md#ga26-p231) |
| 26-68 re "reference" | [Alternate commission member serving as chairman or voting when not needed for quorum](../inquiries/ga26_1998__ci03.md) | The CCB found no constitutional prohibition on an alternate member serving as commission chairman, and ruled that an alternate filling a principal's vacancy may vote even when not needed to constitute a quorum. | BCO 15-1 | advice given — no constitutional prohibition on alternate serving as chairman or voting | Committee of Commissioners on Interchurch Relations | [ga26_1998 p.233](../markdown/ga26_1998.md#ga26-p233) |

## 27th General Assembly (1999)  ·  `ga27_1999`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 27-43 II.1 | [Deacon and session authority to sell donated church property](../inquiries/ga27_1999__ci01.md) | CCB advised that deacons, subject to session approval and congregational consent, hold authority to sell church property such as donated stock. | BCO 9-2, BCO 12-5, BCO 25-6, BCO 25-7, BCO 25-8, BCO 25-10 | advice given | First Presbyterian Church of Montgomery, AL | [ga27_1999 p.144](../markdown/ga27_1999.md#ga27-p144) |
| 27-43 II.2 | [Receiving a missionary candidate unable to appear before presbytery in person](../inquiries/ga27_1999__ci02.md) | The CCB advised that a presbytery may receive a missionary under care via commission or interactive electronic means to satisfy BCO 18-3's appearance requirement. | BCO 18-3 | advice given | James River Presbytery | [ga27_1999 p.145](../markdown/ga27_1999.md#ga27-p145) |
| 27-43 II.3 | [Whether SJC member vows conflict with BCO and Westminster Standards](../inquiries/ga27_1999__ci03.md) | The CCB found SJC vows 4 and 5 'flawed' for failing to reference Scripture directly, and advised amending Vow 4 to resolve the ambiguity with BCO 39-3 and the WCF. | BCO 39-3, RAO 15-1, RAO 17-1 | advice given | Evangel Presbytery | [ga27_1999 p.145](../markdown/ga27_1999.md#ga27-p145) |

## 28th General Assembly (2000)  ·  `ga28_2000`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 28-19 III.2 | [Whether a BCO 34-1 Case May Be Declared Without Being Heard](../inquiries/ga28_2000__ci01.md) | The CCB advised Ascension Presbytery by referring to its contemporaneous answer to Western Carolina Presbytery that a case under BCO 34-1 may be declared without being heard. | BCO 34-1 | advice given | Ascension Presbytery | [ga28_2000 p.70](../markdown/ga28_2000.md#ga28-p68) |
| 28-19 III.2 | [Whether a BCO 34-1 Case May Be Declared Without Being Heard](../inquiries/ga28_2000__ci02.md) | The CCB advised Ascension Presbytery by referring to its contemporaneous answer to Western Carolina Presbytery that a case under BCO 34-1 may be declared without being heard. | BCO 34-1 | advice given | Ascension Presbytery | [ga28_2000 p.70](../markdown/ga28_2000.md#ga28-p70) |
| 28-19 III.3 | [Which GA entities are subject to the permanent committee ineligibility rule](../inquiries/ga28_2000__ci03.md) | The CCB clarified that the "permanent committee" ineligibility provision in RAO 13-2 applies only to the four committees enumerated in RAO 4-2, not to other GA entities. | RAO 4-2, RAO 4-3, RAO 4-4, RAO 13-2, RAO 14-2, BCO 14-1.12 | advice given | Ascension Presbytery | [ga28_2000 p.71](../markdown/ga28_2000.md#ga28-p71) |
| 28-19 III.4 | [Capitalization of 'Church' in BCO 57-5 membership vows](../inquiries/ga28_2000__ci04.md) | The CCB advised James River Presbytery that the original text of BCO 57-5 uses an uppercase 'C' in 'Church' in both membership vows 4 and 5. | BCO 57-5 | advice given | James River Presbytery | [ga28_2000 p.72](../markdown/ga28_2000.md#ga28-p72) |

## 29th General Assembly (2001)  ·  `ga29_2001`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 29-28 III.1 | [Presbytery-wide withdrawal from the General Assembly](../inquiries/ga29_2001__ci01.md) | The Constitution is silent on whether a whole presbytery may withdraw from the GA, though individual congregations may withdraw as a group with each congregation's consent. | BCO 25-11 | advice given | Westminster Presbytery | [ga29_2001 p.133](../markdown/ga29_2001.md#ga29-p133) |
| 29-28 III.2 | [Session authority to raise supermajority voting threshold for officers](../inquiries/ga29_2001__ci02.md) | A Session cannot raise the voting requirement beyond a majority for calling a pastor, dissolving a pastoral relation, or electing a church officer. | BCO 20-4, BCO 24-4 | advice given | Eastern Carolina Presbytery | [ga29_2001 p.134](../markdown/ga29_2001.md#ga29-p134) |
| 29-28 III.3 | [Whether Presbytery Must Divest a Teaching Elder Without Call After Three Years](../inquiries/ga29_2001__ci03.md) | The CCB advised that a presbytery is not required to divest a teaching elder without call after three years, but must inquire and exercise discretion after completing its inquiry. |  | advice given | Southern Florida Presbytery | [ga29_2001 p.134](../markdown/ga29_2001.md#ga29-p134) |
| 29-28 III.4 | [Whether a session may set a minimum age for communicant membership](../inquiries/ga29_2001__ci04.md) | The CCB advised that sessions must evaluate each membership request individually regardless of age, since the timing of credible faith cannot be precisely fixed. | BCO 6-2, BCO 57-2 | advice given | Pittsburgh Presbytery | [ga29_2001 p.134](../markdown/ga29_2001.md#ga29-p134) |
| 29-28 III.5 | [No exceptions permitted in Lord's Supper administration practice](../inquiries/ga29_2001__ci05.md) | The CCB advised Great Lakes Presbytery that BCO 58-4 allows no exception of practice in administering the Lord's Supper, and presbyteries should use BCO 34-5 when considering exceptions from candidates. | BCO 58-4, BCO 34-5 | advice given | Great Lakes Presbytery | [ga29_2001 p.135](../markdown/ga29_2001.md#ga29-p135) |
| 29-28 III.6 | [When a member may lodge a complaint with a higher court](../inquiries/ga29_2001__ci06.md) | The CCB advised that a complainant may not go to a higher court until the lower court has acted on the complaint pursuant to BCO 43-2 and 43-3. | BCO 43-2, BCO 43-3, BCO 43-1 | advice given | Philadelphia Presbytery | [ga29_2001 p.136](../markdown/ga29_2001.md#ga29-p136) |
| 29-28 III.7 | [CCB Authority to Give Unsolicited Advice to Other Committees](../inquiries/ga29_2001__ci07.md) | The CCB advised New River Presbytery that it lacks authority to give unsolicited advice to other committees, though the CRPR may make any recommendation within its RAO 16 purview to the GA. | RAO 14, RAO 16 | advice given | New River Presbytery | [ga29_2001 p.137](../markdown/ga29_2001.md#ga29-p137) |
| 29-28 Item 3 | [General Assembly authority to amend or delete an SJC concurring opinion](../inquiries/ga29_2001__ci09.md) | The CCB advised that the GA may not amend the SJC's report by deleting a concurring opinion, since under BCO 15-5 an SJC decision is the final decision of the Assembly. | BCO 15-5 | advice given |  | [ga29_2001 p.144](../markdown/ga29_2001.md#ga29-p144) |
| 29-28 Item 4 | [SJC authority to opine on legitimacy of General Assembly action](../inquiries/ga29_2001__ci10.md) | The CCB advised that the SJC may only address matters assigned to it, but once assigned a matter may render an opinion on the legitimacy of the GA action that prompted the referral. |  | advice given |  | [ga29_2001 p.145](../markdown/ga29_2001.md#ga29-p145) |
| 29-28 Item 1 | [Temperateness review and status of SJC concurring and dissenting opinions](../inquiries/ga29_2001__ci11.md) | The CCB held that determination of temperateness must occur before a concurring opinion is appended, and that such opinions represent only individual views, not the mind of the Church or binding SJC decisions. | OMSJC 14.7, OMSJC 18.12.b, OMSJC 17.8.k, BCO 14.7, OMSJC 18.3, OMSJC 18.12.a, OMSJC 14-7 | advice given |  | [ga29_2001 p.147](../markdown/ga29_2001.md#ga29-p147) |
| 29-28 Item 2 | [Ordination vows require compliance with Assembly rules of order](../inquiries/ga29_2001__ci12.md) | The CCB advised that delegates are bound by ordination vows to follow PCA constitutional rules, and that changing unscriptural standards requires proper amendment procedures, not suspension. | BCO 21-5.2, BCO 21-5.3, BCO 21-5.4, BCO 21-5.5, BCO 24-6.2, BCO 24-6.3, BCO 24-6.4, BCO 24-6.5, BCO Preface III, BCO 45, BCO 21-5.2-5, BCO 24-6.2-5 | advice given |  | [ga29_2001 p.148](../markdown/ga29_2001.md#ga29-p148) |

## 30th General Assembly (2002)  ·  `ga30_2002`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 30-29 Item 3 | [Constitutional concerns with SJC procedures for hearing memorials](../inquiries/ga30_2002__ci05.md) | The CCB identified significant constitutional issues with the SJC's memorial procedures, including an undefined term, due-process deficiencies, and potential conflict with historical Presbyterian usage. | BCO 40-5 | advice given |  | [ga30_2002 p.104](../markdown/ga30_2002.md#ga30-p104) |
| 30-29 Item B | [Presbytery's right to reclaim a matter from a commission](../inquiries/ga30_2002__ci06.md) | A court may reclaim a matter from a commission before completion, but may not reverse a lower court's decision without actually hearing the appeal per BCO 42. | BCO 42 | advice given | Philadelphia Presbytery | [ga30_2002 p.105](../markdown/ga30_2002.md#ga30-p105) |
| 30-29 Item 2 | [General Assembly authority to mandate health coverage for all active ministers](../inquiries/ga30_2002__ci07.md) | The CCB ruled that neither Scripture nor the constitution grants the General Assembly authority to mandate health insurance purchases, leading the Assembly to substitute a recommendation for a mandate. | BCO 14-1.4 | advice given — not constitutional to mandate |  | [ga30_2002 p.106](../markdown/ga30_2002.md#ga30-p106) |
| 30-29 Item 3 | [Session's right to access congregation's financial and attendance records](../inquiries/ga30_2002__ci08.md) | CCB advised that the session and diaconate have a right to access financial and attendance records as needed, limited only by prudence and protection of reputation. | BCO 8-3, BCO 12-5.a, BCO 12-5.b, WLC 145 | advice given | Southeast Alabama Presbytery | [ga30_2002 p.106](../markdown/ga30_2002.md#ga30-p106) |

## 31st General Assembly (2003)  ·  `ga31_2003`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 31-56 III.1 | [Status of a presbytery case after SJC rules it out of order](../inquiries/ga31_2003__ci01.md) | When the SJC rules a case judicially out of order, the case reverts substantively to the status it held when the presbytery completed its action. |  | advice given | Evangel Presbytery | [ga31_2003 p.159](../markdown/ga31_2003.md#ga31-p159) |
| 31-56 IV | [Whether presbyteries must record ministers' confessional exceptions in minutes](../inquiries/ga31_2003__ci02.md) | The CCB answered no: neither the PCA constitution nor the RAO then required a presbytery to record a minister's exceptions to the Westminster Standards or BCO in its minutes. | RAO 14-3e.5, RAO 16-3.e.5 | advice given — no requirement |  | [ga31_2003 p.159](../markdown/ga31_2003.md#ga31-p159) |
| 31-56 V | [Interpretation of 'papists' in WCF 24.3 for marriage eligibility](../inquiries/ga31_2003__ci11.md) | The CCB declined Iowa Presbytery's reference asking whether 'papists' in WCF 24.3 means all Roman Catholics or only those holding Roman Catholic soteriology, finding no matter pending before the lower court. | BCO 41-5, RAO 7-2(2), RAO 8-2.b.2, BCO 41-4, BCO 41-6, BCO 41-1, WCF 24.3 | declined to accede | Iowa Presbytery | [ga31_2003 p.166](../markdown/ga31_2003.md#ga31-p166) |
| 31-56 III.1-2 | [Presbytery authority to remove censure of suspended or deposed teaching elder](../inquiries/ga31_2003__ci12.md) | A new presbytery may remove censures of suspension or deposition only with the original presbytery's permission or after satisfying BCO 37-7 procedures; the original presbytery retains recourse under BCO 40-3 through 40-5. | BCO 37-7, BCO 34-8, BCO 40-3, BCO 40-4, BCO 40-5 | advice given |  | [ga31_2003 p.167](../markdown/ga31_2003.md#ga31-p167) |

## 32nd General Assembly (2004)  ·  `ga32_2004`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 32-36 II.1 | [Constitutional meaning of "Papists" in WCF 24.3](../inquiries/ga32_2004__ci01.md) | The CCB declined to define "papists" abstractly, leaving the doctrinal question to presbyteries, judicial process, or an in thesi statement. | WCF 24.3 | declined to determine abstractly |  | [ga32_2004 p.132](../markdown/ga32_2004.md#ga32-p132) |
| 32-36 II.2 | [CRPR authority to review presbytery granting of ordination exceptions](../inquiries/ga32_2004__ci02.md) | The CCB advised that a presbytery's granting of exceptions under BCO 21-4 is reviewable by the Committee on Review of Presbytery Records, which must report apparent violations accordingly. | BCO 21-4, RAO 14-6.c, RAO 16-6.c | advice given |  | [ga32_2004 p.133](../markdown/ga32_2004.md#ga32-p133) |
| 32-54 II | [Teaching Elders serving as corporate officers or trustees of a particular church](../inquiries/ga32_2004__ci06.md) | The CCB advised that it is unconstitutional for Teaching Elders to serve as officers or trustees of the corporation of a particular church under BCO 4. | BCO 4 | advice given — unconstitutional |  | [ga32_2004 p.203](../markdown/ga32_2004.md#ga32-p203) |

## 33rd General Assembly (2005)  ·  `ga33_2005`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 33-29 II | [Presbytery membership of a church outside its geographic bounds](../inquiries/ga33_2005__ci04.md) | The CCB advised that a presbytery does not have constitutional authority under BCO 13-1 to include as a member a church located outside its bounds. | BCO 13-1 | advice given — not constitutional | Potomac Presbytery | [ga33_2005 p.155](../markdown/ga33_2005.md#ga33-p156) |

## 34th General Assembly (2006)  ·  `ga34_2006`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 34-34 IV.B | [Use of translations and translators in presbytery ordination examinations](../inquiries/ga34_2006__ci03.md) | Whether translations and translators may be used in presbytery examinations is left to the presbytery's judgment as to whether qualifications are fully satisfied. | BCO 13-6, BCO 18-3, BCO 19-3, BCO 21-4 | advice given | Presbytery of Southern Florida | [ga34_2006 p.81](../markdown/ga34_2006.md#ga34-p82) |
| 34-34 IV.C | [Language in which session records may be written and submitted](../inquiries/ga34_2006__ci04.md) | CCB advised that a presbytery may permit session records kept or translated into English when submitted, provided they meet BCO standards. | BCO 12-7, BCO 13-9b, BCO 40-2 | advice given | Presbytery of Southern Florida | [ga34_2006 p.82](../markdown/ga34_2006.md#ga34-p82) |
| 34-34 IV.D | [Session authority to approve ministerial severance packages](../inquiries/ga34_2006__ci05.md) | CCB advised that sessions may approve severance packages for ministers without congregational approval, as these are budgetary matters under BCO 12-5b. | BCO 12-5b | advice given | Westminster Presbytery | [ga34_2006 p.82](../markdown/ga34_2006.md#ga34-p82) |
| 34-34 IV.E | [Whether teaching that reprobate are in covenant with God is constitutional](../inquiries/ga34_2006__ci06.md) | The CCB declined to answer a doctrinal question from Westminster Presbytery, stating that hypothetical issues of doctrine must be settled by adjudication. |  | advice given — hypothetical issues must be settled by adjudication | Westminster Presbytery | [ga34_2006 p.82](../markdown/ga34_2006.md#ga34-p82) |
| 34-35 IV | [Effect of eliminating 'memorials' terminology on pending SJC cases](../inquiries/ga34_2006__ci07.md) | The CCB advised that the Louisiana Presbytery case, commenced under the old BCO 40-5 as a memorial, must continue to be processed under the old BCO 40-5 and SJC Manual 16 provisions. | BCO 40-5 | advice given |  | [ga34_2006 p.149](../markdown/ga34_2006.md#ga34-p149) |

## 35th General Assembly (2007)  ·  `ga35_2007`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 35-30 IV.B | [CCB declining review of presbytery document on acceptable exceptions](../inquiries/ga35_2007__ci06.md) | The CCB declined a non-judicial reference asking it to review a presbytery's list of acceptable and unacceptable ordination exceptions, citing the complexity of issues better resolved through judicial processes. | BCO 41-5 | declined to accede | Southeast Alabama Presbytery | [ga35_2007 p.76](../markdown/ga35_2007.md#ga35-p76) |

## 36th General Assembly (2008)  ·  `ga36_2008`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 36-30 IV | [Licensing a layman not pursuing a call to elder office](../inquiries/ga36_2008__ci06.md) | The CCB stated that a man not a ruling or teaching elder and not pursuing eldership may nonetheless be licensed to preach per the plain wording of BCO 19-1. | BCO 19-1 | advice given |  | [ga36_2008 p.72](../markdown/ga36_2008.md#ga36-p72) |

## 37th General Assembly (2009)  ·  `ga37_2009`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 37-29 IV.1 | [Session's Responsibility to Examine Children Transferring for Membership](../inquiries/ga37_2009__ci03.md) | The CCB advised that sessions should give appropriate deference to prior admission decisions of other PCA sessions unless compelling reasons exist to do otherwise. | BCO 28-3, BCO 11-4 | advice given | Session of Trinity Presbyterian Church (Susquehanna Presbytery) | [ga37_2009 p.210](../markdown/ga37_2009.md#ga37-p210) |
| 37-29 IV.2 | [Pastoral oversight and discipline when spouses are in different jurisdictions](../inquiries/ga37_2009__ci04.md) | CCB advised that no constitutional obstacle prevents courts from different presbyteries cooperating in marital reconciliation, though the BCO makes no provision for joint commissions of separate courts. | BCO 21-5 | advice given | Missouri Presbytery | [ga37_2009 p.211](../markdown/ga37_2009.md#ga37-p211) |
| 37-29 IV.3 | [Presbytery's investigative duty when a new body holds evangelical beliefs](../inquiries/ga37_2009__ci05.md) | The CCB advised that a presbytery's BCO 2-2 inquiry ends once it concludes a newly formed entity holds a fairly traditional evangelical set of beliefs. | BCO 2-2, BCO 38-3 | advice given | Piedmont Triad Presbytery | [ga37_2009 p.212](../markdown/ga37_2009.md#ga37-p212) |
| 37-29 IV.4 | [Congregational requirement of supermajority vote to elect church officers](../inquiries/ga37_2009__ci06.md) | The PCA Constitution does not permit a congregation to require that candidates for church office receive more than a majority vote to be elected. | BCO 24-5 | advice given | Central Carolina Presbytery | [ga37_2009 p.213](../markdown/ga37_2009.md#ga37-p213) |

## 38th General Assembly (2010)  ·  `ga38_2010`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 38-18 II.1 | [Presbytery discretion in granting exceptions to BCO stated differences](../inquiries/ga38_2010__ci01.md) | The CCB advised that a presbytery may ask candidates to state differences with the BCO but is not required to do so, and the BCO provides no mechanism for formally recognizing such differences. | BCO 21-4 | advice given |  | [ga38_2010 p.122](../markdown/ga38_2010.md#ga38-p122) |
| 38-34 VII | [Ruling elder's participation in SJC case involving his own presbytery](../inquiries/ga38_2010__ci05.md) | The CCB noted an exception where a ruling elder was deemed qualified to participate in reviewing a case involving his own presbytery, contrary to OMSJC 6.2(d). | OMSJC 6.2, OMSJC 2.10.d | exception noted |  | [ga38_2010 p.270](../markdown/ga38_2010.md#ga38-p270) |

## 39th General Assembly (2011)  ·  `ga39_2011`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| App. O V | [Confusing use of 'admonition' in non-judicial rebuke context](../inquiries/ga39_2011__ci02.md) | The CCB cautioned that using 'admonish' and 'admonition' outside their technical judicial-censure meaning in BCO 30 could cause confusion when applied to actions against church officers. | BCO 43-6, BCO 30-1, BCO 30-2, BCO 36-3 | advice given |  | [ga39_2011 p.391](../markdown/ga39_2011.md#ga39-p391) |

## 40th General Assembly (2012)  ·  `ga40_2012`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| App. O IV | [Multisite church polity and scope of session and presbytery oversight](../inquiries/ga40_2012__ci04.md) | The CCB advised that BCO 4 neither prescribes nor proscribes multisite polity, and that presbytery retains review and control over all worship sites, including new ones. | BCO 4 | advice given | Central Carolina Presbytery | [ga40_2012 p.366](../markdown/ga40_2012.md#ga40-p366) |

## 41st General Assembly (2013)  ·  `ga41_2013`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| App. O IV | [Lord's Supper administered to communicants at a separate site](../inquiries/ga41_2013__ci04.md) | The CCB declined to give additional advice to Ohio Valley Presbytery on administering the Lord's Supper at a remote location, directing the matter to other courts of the church. |  | declined to give additional advice | Ohio Valley Presbytery | [ga41_2013 p.364](../markdown/ga41_2013.md#ga41-p364) |

## 42nd General Assembly (2014)  ·  `ga42_2014`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| App. O IV.A | [Defining the scope of "labor" for teaching elders under BCO 13-2](../inquiries/ga42_2014__ci03.md) | The CCB advised that "labor" for teaching elders means needful ministry for Gospel dissemination, with each presbytery determining whether such labor is allowable within its bounds. | BCO 13-2, BCO 8-4, BCO 8-7 | advice given | Evangel Presbytery | [ga42_2014 p.344](../markdown/ga42_2014.md#ga42-p344) |
| App. O IV.B | [Recording candidate's views on male-only eldership at ordination](../inquiries/ga42_2014__ci04.md) | No constitutional procedure exists to record a candidate's views on the BCO's requirements, nor is a candidate required to list his differences with its provisions. | BCO 21-4.c | advice given | Philadelphia Presbytery | [ga42_2014 p.344](../markdown/ga42_2014.md#ga42-p344) |
