| App. O III | [Inactive or non-locally-elected elders as General Assembly commissioners](../inquiries/ga42_2014__ci05.md) | A rotating-session inactive elder may be elected GA commissioner, but a ruling elder never elected in that congregation may not serve as its commissioner. | BCO 7-2, BCO 24-7, BCO 24-9 | advice given | Grace Presbytery | [ga42_2014 p.347](../markdown/ga42_2014.md#ga42-p347) |

## 43rd General Assembly (2015)  ·  `ga43_2015`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| App. O IV.B | [Dual presbytery approval required for an uncalled TE living outside bounds](../inquiries/ga43_2015__ci03.md) | The CCB advised that a TE member living and laboring outside his presbytery's bounds must obtain approval from both his home presbytery and the host presbytery. | BCO 13-2 | advice given | Iowa Presbytery | [ga43_2015 p.373](../markdown/ga43_2015.md#ga43-p372) |
| App. O IV.B | [Dual presbytery approval required for an uncalled TE living outside bounds](../inquiries/ga43_2015__ci04.md) | The CCB advised that a TE member living and laboring outside his presbytery's bounds must obtain approval from both his home presbytery and the host presbytery. | BCO 13-2 | advice given | Iowa Presbytery | [ga43_2015 p.373](../markdown/ga43_2015.md#ga43-p373) |

## 44th General Assembly (2016)  ·  `ga44_2016`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| App. O IV.A | [Propriety of Absentee Ballots in Congregational Meetings](../inquiries/ga44_2016__ci04.md) | The CCB advised that absentee ballots are barred whenever the BCO requires the congregation to convene or a majority of those present to carry a vote. |  | advice given | Great Lakes Presbytery | [ga44_2016 p.348](../markdown/ga44_2016.md#ga44-p348) |
| App. O IV.B | [Scope of presbytery stated clerk's duty to disseminate information](../inquiries/ga44_2016__ci05.md) | CCB advised that a stated clerk may provide information consistent with presbytery records, especially when requested by a former TE in connection with ecclesiastical litigation. | BCO 10-4 | advice given | Korean Southwest Presbytery | [ga44_2016 p.349](../markdown/ga44_2016.md#ga44-p349) |
| App. O IV.C | [Validity of a transferred minister's ordination credentials](../inquiries/ga44_2016__ci06.md) | The CCB advised that the minister in question was properly ordained and was a member in good standing of the presbytery at the time of his transfer. |  | advice given | Korean Southwest Presbytery | [ga44_2016 p.350](../markdown/ga44_2016.md#ga44-p350) |

## 45th General Assembly (2017)  ·  `ga45_2017`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| App. O IV.A | [Stated supply moderator's role in local church discipline proceedings](../inquiries/ga45_2017__ci06.md) | A presbytery-appointed stated supply and session moderator may participate in church discipline but, not being a session member, may not vote. |  | advice given | Blue Ridge Presbytery | [ga45_2017 p.327](../markdown/ga45_2017.md#ga45-p327) |
| App. O IV.B.2 | [Session authority over church budget versus congregational call terms](../inquiries/ga45_2017__ci07.md) | The CCB answered yes: under BCO 12-5.b, the session's duty to approve the budget supersedes the congregation's authority to set pastoral call terms in any fiscal year. | BCO 12-5.b, BCO 20-6 | advice given — yes | New Jersey Presbytery | [ga45_2017 p.329](../markdown/ga45_2017.md#ga45-p329) |
| App. O I.B | [Application of BCO 24-1 examination process to re-election of officers](../inquiries/ga45_2017__ci08.md) | BCO 24-1 applies fully to men with no prior official relationship to the church; for men already serving, lower courts determine which provisions apply at re-election. | BCO 24-1, BCO 24-5, BCO 24-8 | advice given | Presbytery of the Ascension | [ga45_2017 p.330](../markdown/ga45_2017.md#ga45-p330) |

## 47th General Assembly (2019)  ·  `ga47_2019`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| III.A | [SJC member eligibility for concurrent GA agency board service](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci22.md) | The CCB advised that a member of the GA's Standing Judicial Commission is eligible to serve concurrently on the board of a General Assembly agency. | BCO 8-2.a.1, BCO 15-4, BCO 14-1.12, BCO 15-1, RAO 4, RAO 4-3, RAO 4-5, RAO 4-6, RAO 4-10, RAO 4-11, RAO 4-12, RAO 4-13, RAO 4-14, RAO 4-15, RAO 4-16, RAO 4-19, RAO 4-20, RAO 4-21 | Yes (eligible); Adopted by the CCB | Reference from Stated Clerk Dr. Roy Taylor | [ga47_2019 p.402](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p402) |
| III.B | [Consecutive service on different GA committees without sabbatical](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci23.md) | The CCB advised that a GA permanent committee member who completed a full term is not eligible for consecutive service on a different committee or agency board without a sabbatical year. | BCO 8-2.a.1, BCO 14-1.12 | No (not eligible); Adopted by the CCB | Reference from Stated Clerk Dr. Roy Taylor | [ga47_2019 p.403](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p403) |
| III.C | [Effect of presbytery division on advice and consent voting](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci24.md) | The CCB advised that a newly erected presbytery may give advice and consent on BCO amendments already voted on by its predecessor, but individual presbyters may not vote a second time after transfer. | BCO 8-2.a.1, BCO 26 | Yes to (1), No to (2); Adopted by the CCB | Reference from Stated Clerk Dr. Roy Taylor | [ga47_2019 p.403](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p403) |
| V | [Overtures Committee's exclusive authority over constitutional amendments](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci25.md) | The CCB advised that the Overtures Committee has exclusive property over overtures requesting amendment to the Constitution under RAO 11-5. | RAO 11-5 | Yes (exclusive property); Adopted by the CCB | Request from Overtures Committee at 46th General Assembly (June 13, 2018) | [ga47_2019 p.404](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p404) |

## 48th General Assembly (2021)  ·  `ga48_2021`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IV. Non-Judicial Reference 1 | [Procedure for BCO 40-5 reports alleging sessional delinquency](../inquiries/ga48_2021__ci29.md) | CCB issued three opinions addressing procedure, anonymity, and standing when a presbytery receives a BCO 40-5 report alleging delinquency or grossly unconstitutional acts by a Session. | BCO 40-5, BCO 40, BCO 43-1, BCO 31-8 | Answered (three opinions on procedure, anonymity, and standing); Adopted | Reference from Philadelphia Presbytery | [ga48_2021 p.465](../markdown/ga48_2021.md#ga48-p465) |

## 49th General Assembly (2022)  ·  `ga49_2022`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Supplemental III.1 | [Whether Committees of Commissioners may nominate substitute program coordinators](../inquiries/ga49_2022__ci29.md) | CCB ruled that a Committee of Commissioners may only approve or disapprove nominees for program coordinator, and has no authority to nominate substitutes. | RAO 4-9, RAO 20 | CoC may only approve or disapprove, not nominate substitute; Adopted by the CCB | Reference from Stated Clerk regarding floor nominations for Program Coordinators (MNA, MTW, RUF) | [ga49_2022 p.428](../markdown/ga49_2022.md#ga49-p428) |
| Supplemental III.2 | [Floor nomination procedure for the office of Stated Clerk](../inquiries/ga49_2022__ci30.md) | CCB advised that floor nominations for Stated Clerk follow the same procedure as other contested offices, and nominees must be examined by the Theological Examining Committee. | RAO 3-4, RAO 8-4, BCO 14-1, RAO 4-9 | floor nominations follow identical procedure to other contested offices; nominees must be examined by TEC; Adopted by the CCB | Reference from Stated Clerk regarding floor nominations for Stated Clerk | [ga49_2022 p.429](../markdown/ga49_2022.md#ga49-p429) |

## 50th General Assembly (2023)  ·  `ga50_2023`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IV. Non-Judicial Reference 1 | [Whether one presbytery may involuntarily dissociate another presbytery from the PCA](../inquiries/ga50_2023__ci21.md) | The CCB concluded that no procedure exists in the BCO or RAO by which one presbytery may involuntarily dissociate another from the PCA. | BCO 14-6.e, RAO 8-2.B.2 | No such procedure exists; Adopted 8-0-0 | Reference from Grace Presbytery | [ga50_2023 p.459](../markdown/ga50_2023.md#ga50-p459) |

## 52nd General Assembly (2025)  ·  `ga52_2025`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| III.A | [Whether all RAO amendments require CCB review before GA vote](../inquiries/ga52_2025__ci29.md) | The CCB ruled that all RAO amendments require CCB review per RAO 8-2.b.3, but no provision requires Overtures Committee approval for RAO amendments originating from CRPR or the SJC. | RAO 8-2.b.3, RAO 15-1 | All RAO amendments require CCB review per RAO 8-2.b.3; no provision requires Overtures Committee approval for RAO amendments from CRPR or SJC; Adopted 8-0-0 | Request from the Stated Clerk (communication dated May 31, 2024) | [ga52_2025 p.258](../markdown/ga52_2025.md#ga52-p258) |
| III.B | [Timeliness of overtures received by April 25 deadline](../inquiries/ga52_2025__ci30.md) | The CCB ruled that overtures received on or by April 25 are timely under RAO 11-6's 60-day deadline and must be officially handled at the current General Assembly. | RAO 11-6 | Overtures received on or by April 25 are timely per RAO 11-6 (60-day deadline); Adopted 8-0-0 | Request from the Stated Clerk (communication dated April 25, 2025, and in person) | [ga52_2025 p.259](../markdown/ga52_2025.md#ga52-p259) |
| IV.A | [CCB authority to review SJC compliance and prior decisions](../inquiries/ga52_2025__ci31.md) | The CCB declined a reference from Highlands Presbytery, ruling it lacks authority to render opinions on prior SJC decisions, which are final decisions of the General Assembly. | BCO 41-3, BCO 39-3.1, BCO 43-10, BCO 40-2, BCO 15-5 | CCB declined to accede to the reference; SJC decisions are final decisions of the GA (BCO 15-5); CCB does not render opinions on previous SJC decisions; Adopted 8-0-0 | Non-Judicial Reference from Highlands Presbytery (December 6, 2024) | [ga52_2025 p.259](../markdown/ga52_2025.md#ga52-p259) |
| IV.B | [Congregational meeting minutes as part of session records reviewed by presbytery](../inquiries/ga52_2025__ci32.md) | The CCB ruled that congregational meeting minutes are part of session records subject to presbytery review, and sessions must submit them if the presbytery requires it. | BCO 41-3, BCO 40-1, BCO 40, BCO 25-5 | CCB acceded to the reference; Congregational meeting minutes are part of Session records subject to Presbytery review; if Presbytery requires them, Sessions must submit them; Adopted 8-0-0 | Non-Judicial Reference from Platte Valley Presbytery (February 12, 2025) | [ga52_2025 p.260](../markdown/ga52_2025.md#ga52-p260) |
| Supplemental II.1 | [AC Committee of Commissioners authority to nominate a Stated Clerk](../inquiries/ga52_2025__ci34.md) | The CCB ruled that the Administrative Committee's Committee of Commissioners may not make a substitute nomination for Stated Clerk when the matter does not arise from AC recommendations. | RAO 14-6.d, RAO 14-6.h, RAO 14-6.k | No — AC CoC may not make a substitute nomination for Stated Clerk if the matter does not arise in AC recommendations; Adopted 8-0-0 | Request from the Stated Clerk on behalf of the Administrative Committee (communication dated June 2, 2025), Question 1 | [ga52_2025 p.262](../markdown/ga52_2025.md#ga52-p262) |
| Supplemental II.2 | [Floor nominations for Stated Clerk when AC brings no nominee](../inquiries/ga52_2025__ci35.md) | The CCB ruled that floor nominations are permitted under RAO 3-4 even if the Administrative Committee does not bring a Stated Clerk nominee. | RAO 3-4, RAO 4-9, RAO 8-4.i, RAO 13, BCO 14-1.14 | Yes — floor nominations are allowed; RAO 3-4 second sentence provides GA right to select Stated Clerk regardless of AC nominee; Adopted 8-0-0 | Request from the Stated Clerk on behalf of the Administrative Committee (communication dated June 2, 2025), Question 2 | [ga52_2025 p.263](../markdown/ga52_2025.md#ga52-p263) |



==============================================================================
# CCB-OVERTURE-ADVICE.md   (live: https://raymond-rishty.github.io/pca-ga/index/CCB-OVERTURE-ADVICE.md)
==============================================================================

# CCB Advice on Overtures & Proposed Amendments

The **Committee on Constitutional Business (CCB)**'s advice on whether a proposed overture or amendment is *in conflict* with the Constitution (its constitutional review of proposed changes, distinct from answering questions about what the Constitution means). Grouped by Assembly.

Each entry pairs a **Digest-level headnote** (the PCA Digest's editorial summary, Part II) with the **verbatim record** sliced from the minutes; the **Minutes** column deep-links to the source page. **Subject** and **Synopsis** are distilled from the Digest's own text. The roster is drawn from the PCA Digest, Part II (1973–2018); later Assemblies are extracted directly from each volume's CCB report.

*Genuine constitutional inquiries / non-judicial references (questions about the Constitution's meaning) are catalogued separately in **[Constitutional inquiries](INQUIRIES.md)**.*


## 17th General Assembly (1989)  ·  `ga17_1989`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 17-82 III,19 | [Presbytery standing rule exempting retired ministers from reporting](../inquiries/ga17_1989__ci04.md) | Illiana Presbytery's proposed rule relieving retired ministers from mandatory reporting was found not in conflict with BCO 23-2, with the presbytery advised to use its own wisdom under BCO 41-5. | BCO 23-2, BCO 41-5 | not in conflict | Illiana Presbytery | [ga17_1989 p.157](../markdown/ga17_1989.md#ga17-p157) |

## 24th General Assembly (1996)  ·  `ga24_1996`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 24-23 ref. #1 | [Meaning of 'emergency' and validity of supermajority threshold for called presbytery meeting](../inquiries/ga24_1996__ci01.md) | The CCB advised that 'emergency' carries its plain dictionary meaning and that requiring concurrence of six of eight officers before calling a special meeting violates BCO 13-12 by overriding the rights of the smaller group entitled to call such meetings. | BCO 13-12 | advice given — procedure in conflict with BCO 13-12 | Central Georgia Presbytery | [ga24_1996 p.159](../markdown/ga24_1996.md#ga24-p159) |

## 25th General Assembly (1997)  ·  `ga25_1997`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 25-22 IV, 1 | [Perfecting amendment language for removing members from the roll](../inquiries/ga25_1997__ci03.md) | The CCB drafted amended BCO 38-3 language governing roll removal when a member joins another church, with options for retaining or removing names when charges are pending. | BCO 38-3, BCO 45-6, BCO 31-2, BCO 46-5, BCO 38-4 | advice given — proposed amended language for BCO 38-3 forwarded | Potomac Presbytery / Northeast Presbytery | [ga25_1997 p.129](../markdown/ga25_1997.md#ga25-p129) |

## 27th General Assembly (1999)  ·  `ga27_1999`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 27-43 III | [Use of 'appeal' versus 'complaint' in cases without process](../inquiries/ga27_1999__ci04.md) | An overture's final sentence conflicted with the Constitution because appeals lie only in judicial cases; the CCB advised substituting 'complaint' for 'appeal' to cure the conflict. | BCO 38-3 | in conflict | Pittsburgh Presbytery | [ga27_1999 p.146](../markdown/ga27_1999.md#ga27-p146) |
| 27-43 III | [Additional criteria for removing indefinite suspension from office or sacraments](../inquiries/ga27_1999__ci05.md) | A proposed overture adding two criteria beyond BCO 37-3 for removing indefinite suspension was in conflict with the Constitution. | BCO 37-3 | in conflict | Pittsburgh Presbytery | [ga27_1999 p.146](../markdown/ga27_1999.md#ga27-p146) |
| 27-43 III | [Establishing a minimum voting age for congregational meetings](../inquiries/ga27_1999__ci06.md) | The CCB found an overture permitting a minimum voting age in congregational meetings in conflict with the Constitution, particularly BCO 6-4. | BCO 6-4 | in conflict | Pacific Northwest Presbytery | [ga27_1999 p.147](../markdown/ga27_1999.md#ga27-p147) |

## 28th General Assembly (2000)  ·  `ga28_2000`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 28-19 V | [Re-ordination of former PCA ministers who were divested without censure](../inquiries/ga28_2000__ci05.md) | The CCB found no technical constitutional conflict but raised concerns about the nature of ordination and inconsistency between restoration after deposition versus divestiture. | BCO 34-10, BCO 21-5, BCO 36-7, BCO 37-5 | not in technical conflict | Susquehanna Valley Presbytery | [ga28_2000 p.77](../markdown/ga28_2000.md#ga28-p77) |
| 28-19 V | [Disciplinary measures against a presbytery tried on GA floor](../inquiries/ga28_2000__ci06.md) | An overture targeting Tennessee Valley Presbytery conflicted with the Constitution by predetermining guilt and prescribing censures without following BCO 40-4 through 40-6 or referral to the SJC. | BCO 40-4, BCO 40-5, BCO 40-6, BCO 15-1, BCO 15-4 | in conflict | Louisiana Presbytery | [ga28_2000 p.78](../markdown/ga28_2000.md#ga28-p78) |
| 28-19 V | [Constitutional status of the Directory for Worship](../inquiries/ga28_2000__ci07.md) | The CCB issued a majority rationale and minority report providing historical background and differing positions on the constitutional standing of BCO chapters 47–63. | BCO 47, BCO 63 | advice given |  | [ga28_2000 p.80](../markdown/ga28_2000.md#ga28-p80) |

## 29th General Assembly (2001)  ·  `ga29_2001`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 29-28 V | [Including the pastor on a search committee for an associate pastor](../inquiries/ga29_2001__ci08.md) | The CCB found the overture in conflict with the constitution, as teaching elders are not congregational members and may not serve on the congregation's pulpit committee. | BCO 20-2, BCO 20 | in conflict | Susquehanna Valley Presbytery | [ga29_2001 p.139](../markdown/ga29_2001.md#ga29-p139) |

## 30th General Assembly (2002)  ·  `ga30_2002`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 30-29 III | [Proposed overture language for handling exceptions to Westminster Standards](../inquiries/ga30_2002__ci01.md) | The CCB found the proposed overture in conflict with BCO Preliminary Principles, eroding presbyteries' exclusive authority to evaluate candidates' subscription. | BCO Preliminary Principle II.1, BCO Preliminary Principle II.7, BCO Preliminary Principles | in conflict | Ohio Valley Presbytery | [ga30_2002 p.100](../markdown/ga30_2002.md#ga30-p100) |
| 30-29 III | [Overture to abolish the Standing Judicial Commission](../inquiries/ga30_2002__ci02.md) | The CCB found the overture to abolish the SJC in conflict with BCO 14-1 and 15, and also unconstitutionally vague about the basis for alternative decisions. | BCO 14-1, BCO 15, BCO 15-4, BCO 15-5 | in conflict | Westminster Presbytery | [ga30_2002 p.100](../markdown/ga30_2002.md#ga30-p100) |
| 30-29 III | [Proposed Procedure for Resolving Alleged Conflicts Between Scripture and the Constitution](../inquiries/ga30_2002__ci03.md) | The CCB found Eastern Canada Presbytery's overture in conflict with the Constitution because it would allow a General Assembly to bypass constitutional processes and permit modification of commission reports. | BCO 26-1, BCO 26-2, BCO 15-1, BCO 15-5 | in conflict | Eastern Canada Presbytery | [ga30_2002 p.102](../markdown/ga30_2002.md#ga30-p102) |
| 30-29 III | [Mandatory reporting of ordination exceptions to General Assembly](../inquiries/ga30_2002__ci04.md) | An overture requiring presbyteries to record and report all ordination exceptions to the GA was found in conflict with the Constitution because it would allow a presbytery to bind a man's conscience beyond what the BCO permits. | BCO 21-4, BCO Preface II.1, BCO Preface II.7 | in conflict | Westminster Presbytery | [ga30_2002 p.103](../markdown/ga30_2002.md#ga30-p103) |

## 31st General Assembly (2003)  ·  `ga31_2003`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 31-56 VI | [Presbytery authority to record candidates' disagreements with the Constitution](../inquiries/ga31_2003__ci03.md) | The CCB found no conflict, though a minority held that requiring presbyteries to record doctrinal disagreements would erode their exclusive authority to determine subscription under BCO 21-5. | RAO 14-3e.5, RAO 16-3.e.5, BCO 21-5, BCO Preliminary Principle II.2, RAO 14-3, RAO 16-3, RAO 14-8, RAO 16-8, RAO 14-10.b, RAO 16-10.b | not in conflict | Covenant Presbytery | [ga31_2003 p.162](../markdown/ga31_2003.md#ga31-p160) |
| 31-56 VI | [Specifying procedure for handling ordination exceptions in BCO 21-4](../inquiries/ga31_2003__ci04.md) | The CCB majority found an overture specifying exception-handling procedures in conflict with the second ordination vow, though a minority disagreed and found no conflict. | BCO 21-4, BCO 21-5.2 | in conflict (majority); minority reported not in conflict | New Jersey Presbytery | [ga31_2003 p.160](../markdown/ga31_2003.md#ga31-p160) |
| 31-56 VI | [Oversight of temperate language in SJC dissenting opinions](../inquiries/ga31_2003__ci05.md) | The CCB found the overture in conflict with the constitution, as no mechanism exists for commissioners to object to language in a concluded judicial case. |  | in conflict | Central Carolina Presbytery | [ga31_2003 p.161](../markdown/ga31_2003.md#ga31-p161) |
| 31-56 VI | [Applying pastoral-election large-minority rule to ruling elders and deacons](../inquiries/ga31_2003__ci06.md) | Applying BCO 20-5's large-minority provision to the election of ruling elders and deacons conflicts with BCO 24-1 and 24-3. | BCO 20-5, BCO 24-1, BCO 24-3 | in conflict | Eastern Canada Presbytery | [ga31_2003 p.162](../markdown/ga31_2003.md#ga31-p162) |
| 31-56 VI | [Presbytery authority to record candidates' disagreements with the Constitution](../inquiries/ga31_2003__ci07.md) | The CCB found no conflict, though a minority held that requiring presbyteries to record doctrinal disagreements would erode their exclusive authority to determine subscription under BCO 21-5. | RAO 14-3e.5, RAO 16-3.e.5, BCO 21-5, BCO Preliminary Principle II.2, BCO Principle II.2 | not in conflict | Covenant Presbytery | [ga31_2003 p.162](../markdown/ga31_2003.md#ga31-p162) |
| 31-56 VI | [GA Stated Clerk reporting exceptions taken in presbyteries on ordination](../inquiries/ga31_2003__ci08.md) | The overture was mostly not in conflict with the Constitution, but a final sentence risked eroding presbyteries' authority to determine confessional subscription. | BCO 24-1, BCO 21-5 | not in conflict | Westminster Presbytery | [ga31_2003 p.162](../markdown/ga31_2003.md#ga31-p162) |
| 31-56 VI | [Restricting presbytery and General Assembly voting to pastors only](../inquiries/ga31_2003__ci09.md) | The CCB found the overture in conflict with BCO 3-1, which vests church power in the whole body, and BCO 14-2, which entitles all TEs to representation. | BCO 3-1, BCO 13-1, BCO 14-2, BCO 23-2, BCO 24-9 | in conflict | Grace Presbytery | [ga31_2003 p.163](../markdown/ga31_2003.md#ga31-p163) |
| 31-18 | [Overtures Committee authority to offer germane amendments to overtures](../inquiries/ga31_2003__ci10.md) | The CCB found that permitting the Bills and Overtures Committee to amend overtures conflicted with the constitution by undermining presbyteries' right to propose measures to the Assembly. | RAO 13-5, RAO 13-6, RAO 15-6 | in conflict | Ascension Presbytery | [ga31_2003 p.67](../markdown/ga31_2003.md#ga31-p67) |

## 32nd General Assembly (2004)  ·  `ga32_2004`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 32-36 III | [RAO amendment omitting presbytery responses on ministers' stated differences](../inquiries/ga32_2004__ci03.md) | The CCB found the proposed RAO amendment in conflict with BCO 21-4 because its reporting requirements failed to cover all possible presbytery responses to candidates' stated differences with the Standards. | BCO 21-4, RAO 14-3.e.5, RAO 16-3.3.5 | in conflict |  | [ga32_2004 p.133](../markdown/ga32_2004.md#ga32-p133) |
| 32-40 II.D | [Limiting TE voting rights in presbytery and General Assembly to pastors](../inquiries/ga32_2004__ci04.md) | The CCB again found the overture in conflict with BCO 3-1 and 14-2, reaffirming that restricting voting to pastors disenfranchises non-pastor Teaching Elders. | BCO 3-1, BCO 13-1, BCO 14-2, BCO 23-2, BCO 24-9 | in conflict | Session of Ellisville Presbyterian Church | [ga32_2004 p.139](../markdown/ga32_2004.md#ga32-p139) |
| 32-40 II.G | [Confessional subscription and amendment of exception-stating requirements](../inquiries/ga32_2004__ci05.md) | An overture from Grace Presbytery amending BCO Preface II, chapter 16, and BCO 21-4 regarding doctrinal subscription was found in conflict with the second ordination vow. | BCO Preface II, BCO 16, BCO 21-4, BCO 21-5.2, BCO 24-5.2, BCO 24-6.2 | in conflict | Grace Presbytery | [ga32_2004 p.140](../markdown/ga32_2004.md#ga32-p140) |

## 33rd General Assembly (2005)  ·  `ga33_2005`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 33-29 II.A | [Proposed annotation to WCF defining term "Papist"](../inquiries/ga33_2005__ci01.md) | The CCB ruled an overture to annotate the WCF to define "Papist" out of order, as no constitutional process or precedent exists for WCF annotations. | WCF | not in order | Iowa Presbytery | [ga33_2005 p.154](../markdown/ga33_2005.md#ga33-p154) |
| 33-29 II.C | [Relationship of assistant pastor to congregation under BCO 22](../inquiries/ga33_2005__ci02.md) | An overture detailing how assistant pastors are called was found in conflict with BCO 3-2 and 12-2 through 12-4, as the relationship of an assistant pastor to a congregation is best left to the session. | BCO 3-2, BCO 12-2, BCO 12-3, BCO 12-4, BCO 22-3, BCO 22-4, BCO 21-5, BCO 21-6, BCO 21-7, BCO 21-8, BCO 21-10 | in conflict | Chesapeake Presbytery | [ga33_2005 p.154](../markdown/ga33_2005.md#ga33-p154) |
| 33-29 II.D | [Mandate requiring teaching elders to explain approved exceptions](../inquiries/ga33_2005__ci03.md) | An overture requiring teaching elders to explain all approved exceptions was found in conflict with BCO Preliminary Principle 1 and WCF 20, as it could compel a man to teach what he does not believe. | BCO 21-4, BCO Preface I, WCF 20 | in conflict | Ascension Presbytery | [ga33_2005 p.154](../markdown/ga33_2005.md#ga33-p154) |
| 33-29 II | [Committing all disciplinary matters to the Standing Judicial Commission](../inquiries/ga33_2005__ci05.md) | A proposed amendment to BCO 15-4 committing all matters governed by the Rules of Discipline to the SJC was found not in conflict with the constitution. | BCO 15-4 | not in conflict |  | [ga33_2005 p.156](../markdown/ga33_2005.md#ga33-p156) |

## 34th General Assembly (2006)  ·  `ga34_2006`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 34-34 II.C | [Overture requiring all BCO changes referred to CCB and Bills and Overtures](../inquiries/ga34_2006__ci01.md) | The CCB found this overture in conflict with RAO 13-1 and BCO 14-1, which permit BCO amendments of a particular nature to be referred to other committees and allow proposals to arise from other Assembly reports. | RAO 10-5, RAO 11-5, RAO 13-1, BCO 14-1, BCO 14-1.15 | in conflict | James River Presbytery | [ga34_2006 p.80](../markdown/ga34_2006.md#ga34-p80) |
| 34-34 II.F | [Mandating explanation of approved doctrinal exceptions by teaching elders](../inquiries/ga34_2006__ci02.md) | A second overture on the same topic was found in conflict because it would compel a man to explain—or appear not to hold—a view his presbytery has already judged non-vital and approved. | BCO 21-4, BCO Preface I, WCF 20 | in conflict | Presbytery of the Ascension | [ga34_2006 p.81](../markdown/ga34_2006.md#ga34-p81) |

## 35th General Assembly (2007)  ·  `ga35_2007`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 35-30 II.B | [Overture mandating sprinkling as sole permissible baptismal mode](../inquiries/ga35_2007__ci01.md) | A Presbytery of New Jersey overture to alter BCO 56-4.d was in conflict with WCF 28.3, which permits multiple modes of baptism, as the proposed language would allow only sprinkling. | BCO 56-4.d, WCF 28.3 | in conflict | Presbytery of New Jersey | [ga35_2007 p.71](../markdown/ga35_2007.md#ga35-p72) |
| 35-30 II.C | [Presbytery authority to decline approval of a pastoral call](../inquiries/ga35_2007__ci02.md) | The CCB majority found an overture revising BCO 21-1 on a presbytery's right to decline pastoral calls not in conflict with the Constitution, while a minority concluded it was in conflict. | BCO 21-1 | not in conflict (majority); minority reported in conflict | Westminster Presbytery | [ga35_2007 p.72](../markdown/ga35_2007.md#ga35-p72) |
| 35-30 II.H | [Restricting presbytery voting rights to financially contributing churches](../inquiries/ga35_2007__ci03.md) | The CCB found that a proposed overture conditioning presbytery voting on church financial contributions was not in conflict with the constitution, though a minority dissented. | BCO 13-1, BCO 14-2 | not in conflict | Session of Alexandria (VA) Presbyterian Church | [ga35_2007 p.74](../markdown/ga35_2007.md#ga35-p74) |
| 35-30 II.I | [Presbytery discretion restricted by mandatory teaching of all exceptions](../inquiries/ga35_2007__ci04.md) | An overture requiring a presbytery to allow a candidate to teach all his exceptions before approving him was found in conflict, as it violated presbytery's right to set ordination qualifications. | BCO 21-4, BCO Preface II | in conflict | Potomac Presbytery | [ga35_2007 p.75](../markdown/ga35_2007.md#ga35-p75) |
| 35-30 IV.A | [Presbytery standing rule banning persons removed from a PCA church](../inquiries/ga35_2007__ci05.md) | The CCB opined that a proposed presbytery standing rule extending a church property ban to presbytery events was not in conflict with the PCA constitution. | WCF 23-3 | not in conflict | Tennessee Valley Presbytery | [ga35_2007 p.75](../markdown/ga35_2007.md#ga35-p75) |

## 36th General Assembly (2008)  ·  `ga36_2008`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 36-30 II.B | [Requiring Apostles' Creed affirmation for church membership](../inquiries/ga36_2008__ci01.md) | A Southeast Alabama overture requiring Apostles' Creed affirmation for membership was in conflict because 'profession of faith' in the BCO denotes commitment to Christ, not subscription to a doctrinal statement. | BCO 57-5 | in conflict | Southeast Alabama Presbytery | [ga36_2008 p.68](../markdown/ga36_2008.md#ga36-p68) |
| 36-30 II.C | [Proposed membership-transfer vows misusing 'profession of faith'](../inquiries/ga36_2008__ci02.md) | A Southeast Alabama overture adding transfer-of-membership procedures was in conflict because it equated 'profession of faith' with doctrinal subscription and contained internal procedural contradictions. | BCO 38-3a, BCO 46-5, BCO 46-6, BCO 57-6 | in conflict | Southeast Alabama Presbytery | [ga36_2008 p.68](../markdown/ga36_2008.md#ga36-p68) |
| 36-30 II.E | [Suspending a Teaching Elder During a Presbytery Investigation](../inquiries/ga36_2008__ci03.md) | The CCB found Missouri Presbytery's overture to amend investigative procedures for a teaching elder was not in constitutional conflict, though a minority report argued otherwise. | BCO 31-2 | not in conflict | Missouri Presbytery | [ga36_2008 p.69](../markdown/ga36_2008.md#ga36-p69) |
| 36-30 II.F | [Erecting a commission for investigation before process is instituted](../inquiries/ga36_2008__ci04.md) | The overture was in conflict with BCO 31-2 and 32-2, which make clear that a judicial case does not exist until process is actually instituted. | BCO 15-3, BCO 31-2, BCO 32-2 | in conflict | Missouri Presbytery | [ga36_2008 p.71](../markdown/ga36_2008.md#ga36-p71) |
| 36-30 II.J | [Ad interim committee to revise the RAO and its funding](../inquiries/ga36_2008__ci05.md) | The CCB found the overture to form an ad interim committee to revise the RAO in conflict with RAO 9-2 on the issue of funding. | RAO 9-2 | in conflict | Potomac Presbytery | [ga36_2008 p.72](../markdown/ga36_2008.md#ga36-p72) |

## 37th General Assembly (2009)  ·  `ga37_2009`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 37-29 II.C | [Higher court assumption of original jurisdiction before lower court concludes](../inquiries/ga37_2009__ci01.md) | A proposed amendment allowing a higher court to intervene when the lower court has had reasonable time was found to conflict with BCO 11-3's restriction against impinging on lower court authority. | BCO 34-1, BCO 33-1, BCO 11-3 | in conflict | Central Carolina Presbytery | [ga37_2009 p.208](../markdown/ga37_2009.md#ga37-p208) |
| 37-29 II.H | [Mandatory prior notice requirement for congregational withdrawal from PCA](../inquiries/ga37_2009__ci02.md) | A proposed notice requirement for withdrawal conflicted with BCO 25-11, which allows a church to withdraw at any time for reasons it deems sufficient. | BCO 25-11, BCO 25-12 | in conflict | North Texas Presbytery | [ga37_2009 p.208](../markdown/ga37_2009.md#ga37-p210) |

## 38th General Assembly (2010)  ·  `ga38_2010`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 38-34 II.A | [Constitutional conflict in overture prohibiting deaconesses](../inquiries/ga38_2010__ci02.md) | CCB found an overture prohibiting deaconesses in conflict with the Constitution because 'deaconess' need not denote an ordained office equivalent to deacon. | BCO 9-7 | in conflict | Central Carolina Presbytery | [ga38_2010 p.276](../markdown/ga38_2010.md#ga38-p267) |
| 38-34 II.D | [Constitutional conflict in overture prohibiting diaconal assistants](../inquiries/ga38_2010__ci03.md) | CCB found an overture prohibiting commissioned or installed assistants to deacons in conflict with the Constitution on the same grounds as the deaconess overture. | BCO 9-7 | in conflict | Eastern Carolina Presbytery | [ga38_2010 p.276](../markdown/ga38_2010.md#ga38-p268) |
| 38-34 II.E | [Appointing unordained men and women to diaconal ministry roles](../inquiries/ga38_2010__ci04.md) | CCB found an overture appointing unordained persons to diaconal ministry in conflict with the Constitution, including because it implied an unordained office contrary to BCO 17-1. | BCO 1-4, BCO 4-2, BCO 5-10, BCO 7-2, BCO 9-2, BCO 9-7, BCO 9-8, BCO 17-1 | in conflict | Northern California Presbytery | [ga38_2010 p.276](../markdown/ga38_2010.md#ga38-p268) |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| App. O II.E | [Temporary governance for churches lacking ruling elders](../inquiries/ga39_2011__ci01.md) | The CCB found that a proposed overture on temporary governance for elder-less churches may have conflicted with the constitution if read as prescriptive under BCO Preliminary Principles 2 and 6 and BCO 12-1. | BCO Preliminary Principle 2, BCO Preliminary Principle 6, BCO 12-1 | may have been in conflict | New Jersey Presbytery | [ga39_2011 p.390](../markdown/ga39_2011.md#ga39-p390) |

## 40th General Assembly (2012)  ·  `ga40_2012`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| App. O II.K | [Clarifying What Triggers a Required Investigative Inquiry Under BCO 31-2](../inquiries/ga40_2012__ci01.md) | The CCB found Pacific Northwest Presbytery's overture clarifying investigation triggers not in conflict, though a dissent warned it could mandate inquiry even on slight grounds. | BCO 31-2, BCO 34-2 | not in conflict | Pacific Northwest Presbytery | [ga40_2012 p.364](../markdown/ga40_2012.md#ga40-p364) |
| App. O II.S | [Restricting removal of excommunication to the original court](../inquiries/ga40_2012__ci02.md) | An overture requiring only the original session to remove excommunication conflicted with BCO 37-7, which allows a court with current jurisdiction to act after a member relocates. | BCO 37-4, BCO 37-7 | in conflict | Westminster Presbytery | [ga40_2012 p.365](../markdown/ga40_2012.md#ga40-p365) |
| App. O II.T | [Sufficiency of faith profession for visible church membership](../inquiries/ga40_2012__ci03.md) | CCB found proposed overtures conflicting with BCO because the only required profession for church membership is faith in Jesus Christ, with the session judging qualifications. | BCO 1-3, BCO 2-1, BCO 6-2, BCO 57-2, BCO 38-3a, BCO 46-6, BCO 57-5, BCO 57-6 | in conflict | Southeast Alabama Presbytery | [ga40_2012 p.365](../markdown/ga40_2012.md#ga40-p365) |

## 41st General Assembly (2013)  ·  `ga41_2013`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| App. O II.D | [Defining Supporting Reasons Required for a Complaint or Appeal](../inquiries/ga41_2013__ci01.md) | The CCB found Suncoast Florida Presbytery's overture adding a definition of supporting reasons for complaints or appeals was not in conflict if BCO 32-18 is read as addressing cases in process. | BCO 32-21, BCO 32-18 | not in conflict | Suncoast Florida Presbytery | [ga41_2013 p.362](../markdown/ga41_2013.md#ga41-p362) |
| App. O II.E | [Proposed definition of terms in BCO 42 narrowing appeal grounds](../inquiries/ga41_2013__ci02.md) | An overture adding a definitions section to BCO 42 was possibly in conflict with BCO 42-3 if the new definitions excluded grounds of appeal already recognized there. | BCO 42-13, BCO 42-3 | in conflict | Suncoast Florida Presbytery | [ga41_2013 p.362](../markdown/ga41_2013.md#ga41-p362) |
| App. O II.F | [Proposed BCO 43 definitions improperly narrowing complaint scope](../inquiries/ga41_2013__ci03.md) | A Suncoast Florida overture to define terms in BCO 43 was found in conflict because it would restrict complaints to judicial-case matters, contradicting BCO 43-1's broader 'any act or decision' standard. | BCO 43-11, BCO 43-1 | in conflict | Suncoast Florida Presbytery | [ga41_2013 p.362](../markdown/ga41_2013.md#ga41-p362) |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| App. O II.F | [General Assembly authority to review and vote on SJC judicial decisions](../inquiries/ga42_2014__ci01.md) | The CCB found an overture allowing the Assembly to vote on SJC decisions was in conflict with the Constitution and also assigned tasks beyond the CCB's own purview under RAO 8.2.b. | BCO 15-5.a, BCO 15-5.b, RAO 8.2.b | in conflict | Calvary Presbytery | [ga42_2014 p.343](../markdown/ga42_2014.md#ga42-p343) |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

| App. O II.E | [Requiring ministers without call to report annually to presbytery](../inquiries/ga43_2015__ci02.md) | An overture mandating annual presbytery reporting for officers without call was in conflict with the Constitution because the new BCO 34-10 language conflicted with BCO 42-2. | BCO 13-2, BCO 34-10, BCO 24-7, BCO 24-9, BCO 42-2 | in conflict | Tidewater Presbytery | [ga43_2015 p.372](../markdown/ga43_2015.md#ga43-p372) |

## 44th General Assembly (2016)  ·  `ga44_2016`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| App. O II.B | [General Assembly power to give directions to SJC on judicial decisions](../inquiries/ga44_2016__ci01.md) | The CCB found an overture allowing the Assembly to direct SJC judicial reasoning was in conflict with the Constitution, because a commission concludes business as the court itself under BCO 15. | BCO 15-5.a, RAO 17-1, BCO 15, BCO 15-4 | in conflict | Pittsburgh Presbytery | [ga44_2016 p.346](../markdown/ga44_2016.md#ga44-p346) |
| App. O II.G | [Requirement to submit and review minutes of a mission church evangelist](../inquiries/ga44_2016__ci02.md) | The CCB found the overture in conflict with BCO 5-3.a because an evangelist, unlike governing bodies, has no minutes to submit under the BCO. | BCO 5-3, BCO 5-3.a | in conflict | Pacific Northwest Presbytery | [ga44_2016 p.348](../markdown/ga44_2016.md#ga44-p348) |
| App. O II.I | [Overture precluding complaints while judicial case is pending](../inquiries/ga44_2016__ci03.md) | A Pacific Northwest overture to clarify timing of higher-court complaint review was in conflict because it would bar filing any complaint against a court while a judicial case is in process. | BCO 43-1 | in conflict | Pacific Northwest Presbytery | [ga44_2016 p.348](../markdown/ga44_2016.md#ga44-p348) |

## 45th General Assembly (2017)  ·  `ga45_2017`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| App. O II.A | [Granting BCO chapter 59 on marriage full constitutional status](../inquiries/ga45_2017__ci01.md) | A Calvary Presbytery overture to elevate BCO 59 to full constitutional status was in conflict because doing so is equivalent to amending the BCO and requires the same multi-stage approval process under BCO 26-2. | BCO 59, BCO 26-2 | in conflict | Calvary Presbytery | [ga45_2017 p.324](../markdown/ga45_2017.md#ga45-p324) |
| App. O II.B | [Adding male-only ordination language as fundamental to system of doctrine](../inquiries/ga45_2017__ci02.md) | The overture was in conflict because designating male-only ordination an 'essential component' of ecclesiology improperly added it to the 'fundamental to our system of doctrine' standard. | BCO 24-11, BCO 21-4.e, BCO 21-4.f | in conflict | Northwest Georgia Presbytery | [ga45_2017 p.325](../markdown/ga45_2017.md#ga45-p325) |
| App. O II.G | [Sabbatical provisions for ruling elders and deacons under BCO 24-7](../inquiries/ga45_2017__ci03.md) | The proposed overture was in conflict because its use of 'officer' was broader than the definition of ruling elders and deacons in BCO 7-2. | BCO 24-7, BCO 7-2 | in conflict | Suncoast Florida Presbytery | [ga45_2017 p.326](../markdown/ga45_2017.md#ga45-p326) |
| App. O II.H | [Directing clerk to refer overtures on committees and agencies to Overtures Committee](../inquiries/ga45_2017__ci04.md) | The CCB found the proposed RAO revision created ambiguity and could produce conflicting recommendations from two committees with no RAO procedure to resolve such floor conflicts. | RAO 11-5 | created ambiguity | Western Carolina Presbytery | [ga45_2017 p.326](../markdown/ga45_2017.md#ga45-p326) |
| App. O II.N | [Limiting formation of ad interim committees to presbytery overtures only](../inquiries/ga45_2017__ci05.md) | The CCB found the overture in conflict with RAO 9-4, as the phrase 'exclusively submitted' directly contradicted existing provisions permitting other means of forming ad interim committees. | RAO 9-4, RAO 9-5 | in conflict | James River Presbytery | [ga45_2017 p.327](../markdown/ga45_2017.md#ga45-p327) |

## 46th General Assembly (2018)  ·  `ga46_2018`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| App. O II.S | [Granting BCO 59 on Marriage Full Constitutional Status](../inquiries/ga46_2018__ci01.md) | The CCB found Overture 24 from Tennessee Valley Presbytery, which sought to grant BCO 59's marriage provisions full constitutional status, to be in conflict with the Constitution. | BCO 59 | in conflict | Tennessee Valley Presbytery | [ga46_2018 p.320](../markdown/ga46_2018.md#ga46-p316) |
| App. O II.E | [Proposed Revision of BCO 59 Marriage Language and Consent](../inquiries/ga46_2018__ci02.md) | The CCB found Calvary Presbytery's overture to revise BCO 59 in conflict with the Constitution, as proposed changes to marriage publication and consent conflicted with BCO Preliminary Principle 7, BCO 11-2, BCO 29-1, and WCF 20.2. | BCO 59-2, BCO 59-5, BCO Preliminary Principle 7, BCO 11-2, BCO 29-1, WCF 20.2, WCF 24.3, BCO Preface III, BCO 26-2 | in conflict | Calvary Presbytery | [ga46_2018 p.317](../markdown/ga46_2018.md#ga46-p317) |
| App. O II.F | [Routing Ad Interim Committee Recommendations Through Overtures Committee](../inquiries/ga46_2018__ci03.md) | The CCB found Calvary Presbytery's overture requiring ad interim committee recommendations to go through the Overtures Committee in conflict with BCO 14-1.15 and productive of procedural ambiguity on the GA floor. | BCO 14-1.15 | in conflict | Calvary Presbytery | [ga46_2018 p.317](../markdown/ga46_2018.md#ga46-p317) |
| App. O II.H | [Overture defining roles of unordained deaconesses and deacon assistants](../inquiries/ga46_2018__ci04.md) | CCB found Grace Presbytery's overture on deaconesses and women officers in conflict with the Constitution, noting the session, not the congregation, is the appointing body requiring vows. | BCO 9-7, BCO 11-2, BCO 24-11, BCO Preliminary Principle 7, BCO 26-2 | in conflict | Grace Presbytery | [ga46_2018 p.318](../markdown/ga46_2018.md#ga46-p318) |
| App. O II.O | [Thirty-Day Notice for Church Withdrawal and Congregational Competency](../inquiries/ga46_2018__ci05.md) | The CCB found Western Canada Presbytery's overture requiring thirty days' notice to leave the PCA was not in conflict, but a minority argued it violated congregational competency and civil sufficiency clauses. | BCO 25-11 | not in conflict | Western Canada Presbytery | [ga46_2018 p.319](../markdown/ga46_2018.md#ga46-p318) |
| App. O II.O | [Thirty-Day Notice for Church Withdrawal and Congregational Competency](../inquiries/ga46_2018__ci06.md) | The CCB found Western Canada Presbytery's overture requiring thirty days' notice to leave the PCA was not in conflict, but a minority argued it violated congregational competency and civil sufficiency clauses. | BCO 25-11 | not in conflict | Western Canada Presbytery | [ga46_2018 p.319](../markdown/ga46_2018.md#ga46-p319) |
| App. O II.P | [Definite Suspension as Conclusion of Judicial Process Under BCO 30](../inquiries/ga46_2018__ci07.md) | The CCB found Philadelphia Presbytery's overture on definite suspension in conflict with BCO 30-1, which states that definite suspension concludes the judicial process. | BCO 30-3, BCO 37-1, BCO 30-1 | in conflict | Philadelphia Presbytery | [ga46_2018 p.319](../markdown/ga46_2018.md#ga46-p319) |
| App. O II.S | [Granting BCO 59 on Marriage Full Constitutional Status](../inquiries/ga46_2018__ci08.md) | The CCB found Overture 24 from Tennessee Valley Presbytery, which sought to grant BCO 59's marriage provisions full constitutional status, to be in conflict with the Constitution. | BCO 59 | in conflict | Tennessee Valley Presbytery | [ga46_2018 p.320](../markdown/ga46_2018.md#ga46-p320) |

## 47th General Assembly (2019)  ·  `ga47_2019`

| Inquiry | Subject | Synopsis | Provisions | Outcome | From | Minutes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| II.A | [Permitting additional ruling elder representation at General Assembly](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci01.md) | The CCB found an overture from Northwest Georgia Presbytery to revise BCO 14-2 permitting additional ruling elder representation at the General Assembly was not in conflict with the Constitution. | BCO 14-2 | not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB | Overture 1 from Northwest Georgia Presbytery | [ga47_2019 p.398](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p398) |
| II.B | [Releasing Covenant Theological Seminary from General Assembly oversight](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci02.md) | The CCB found an overture encouraging CTS toward greater growth and release from Assembly oversight created a conflict with the RAO and Corporate By-Laws. | RAO, Corporate By-Laws | creates conflict; Adopted by the CCB | Overture 2 from Northwest Georgia Presbytery | [ga47_2019 p.398](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p398) |
| II.C | [Promoting godly prudence in corporate prayer and worship at General Assembly](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci03.md) | The CCB found an overture amending RAO 10-3 to encourage godly prudence in planning corporate prayer and worship at the General Assembly was not in conflict with the Constitution. | RAO 10-3 | not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB | Overture 3 from Northern New England Presbytery | [ga47_2019 p.398](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p398) |
| II.D | [Procedures for dissolving various pastoral relations under BCO 23-1](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci04.md) | The CCB found an overture from Central Indiana Presbytery amending BCO 23-1 on procedures for dissolving pastoral relations was not in conflict with the Constitution. | BCO 23-1, BCO 20-3, BCO 20-4 | not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB | Overture 5 from Central Indiana Presbytery | [ga47_2019 p.398](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p398) |
| II.E | [Proposal to disallow memorials via RAO amendment](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci05.md) | An overture from Pacific Northwest Presbytery to amend RAO 11 and 13 to disallow memorials was found not in conflict and adopted by the CCB. | RAO 11, RAO 13 | not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB | Overture 6 from Pacific Northwest Presbytery | [ga47_2019 p.399](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p399) |
| II.F | [Ad interim committee on domestic abuse and sexual assault](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci06.md) | An overture to form an ad interim committee addressing domestic abuse, domestic oppression, and sexual assault was found in conflict with RAO 9-3 but adopted by the CCB. | RAO 9-3 | in conflict; Adopted by the CCB | Overture 7 from Pacific Northwest Presbytery (Revised) | [ga47_2019 p.399](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p399) |
| II.G | [Allowing non-ordained persons on GA committees and boards](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci07.md) | An Ohio Valley Presbytery overture to amend BCO 14-1 to permit non-ordained persons to serve on GA committees and boards was found in conflict and adopted by the CCB. | BCO 14-1.10, BCO 14-1.11, BCO 14-1.12, RAO 5-2, RAO 4-21.c.6, RAO 4-5 | in conflict; Adopted by the CCB | Overture 8 from Ohio Valley Presbytery | [ga47_2019 p.399](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p399) |
| II.H | [Amending filing methods and deadlines for judicial cases](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci08.md) | An overture amending BCO 42-4, 43-2, and 43-3 to update methods and deadlines for filing judicial cases was found not in conflict and adopted by the CCB. | BCO 42-4, BCO 43-2, BCO 43-3 | not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB | Overture 9 from Platte Valley Presbytery | [ga47_2019 p.399](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p399) |
| II.I | [Floor nominations procedure under RAO 8-4](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci09.md) | An overture from Northern New England Presbytery to amend RAO 8-4 regarding floor nominations was found not in conflict and adopted by the CCB. | RAO 8-4 | not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB | Overture 12 from Northern New England Presbytery | [ga47_2019 p.399](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p399) |
| II.J | [Non-ordained persons on committees and boards, second version](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci10.md) | A Southern New England Presbytery version of the overture to allow non-ordained persons on GA committees and boards was found in conflict and adopted by the CCB. | BCO 14-1.10, BCO 14-1.11, BCO 14-1.12, RAO 5-2, RAO 4-21.c.6, RAO 4-5 | in conflict; Adopted by the CCB | Overture 14 from Southern New England Presbytery | [ga47_2019 p.400](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p400) |
| II.K | [Preventing contradictory overture actions by multiple commissioner committees](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci11.md) | An overture to amend RAO 11-5 to prevent multiple committees of commissioners from taking contradictory actions on overtures was found to create internal conflict and was adopted by the CCB. | RAO 11-5 | creates internal conflict; Adopted by the CCB | Overture 15 from West Hudson Presbytery | [ga47_2019 p.400](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p400) |
| II.L | [Creating offices of Moderator-Elect and Vice-Moderator](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci12.md) | An overture from Georgia Foothills Presbytery to amend the RAO to provide for a Moderator-Elect and Vice-Moderator was found not in conflict and adopted by the CCB. | RAO 1-4, RAO 1-6, RAO 1-7, RAO 1-8, RAO 2-1, RAO 2-3 | not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB | Overture 16 from Georgia Foothills Presbytery | [ga47_2019 p.400](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p400) |
| II.M | [Permitting video testimony by witnesses in judicial process](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci13.md) | A Houston Metro Presbytery overture to amend BCO 32-8 to allow video testimony by witnesses in church judicial proceedings was found in conflict and adopted by the CCB. | BCO 32-8, BCO 35-10, BCO 32-13 | in conflict; Adopted by the CCB | Overture 17 from Houston Metro Presbytery | [ga47_2019 p.400](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p400) |
| II.N | [Conflicting overture actions by commissioner committees, second filing](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci14.md) | A Houston Metro Presbytery overture to amend RAO 11-5 to prevent contradictory actions on overtures by multiple committees of commissioners was found to create internal conflict and adopted by the CCB. | RAO 11-5 | creates internal conflict; Adopted by the CCB | Overture 18 from Houston Metro Presbytery | [ga47_2019 p.400](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p400) |

| II.Q | [Local session discretion to permit women serving as deacons](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci17.md) | This overture proposed amending multiple BCO provisions to allow individual sessions to decide locally whether women may serve as deacons; the CCB found it not in conflict. | BCO 5-9.c, BCO 5-9.i(1), BCO 7-2, BCO 9-3, BCO 17-3, BCO 24, BCO 25 | not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB | Overture 29 from Metro New York Presbytery | [ga47_2019 p.401](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p401) |

| II.S | [Adding a Trinitarian vow to ordination vows](../inquiries/ga47_2019__ci19.md) | This overture proposing to add a vow regarding the Trinity to BCO 57-5 was found not in conflict with the Constitution. | BCO 57-5 | not in conflict; Adopted by the CCB | Overture 33 from Tennessee Valley Presbytery | [ga47_2019 p.402](../markdown/ga47_2019.md#ga47-p402) |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

| II.P | [Ordination ban for men self-identifying as gay Christians](../inquiries/ga48_2021__ci16.md) | The CCB found this overture to amend BCO 17 prohibiting ordination of men using gay-identity labels to be in conflict with existing BCO provisions, though it was adopted. | BCO 17, BCO 12-5.b, BCO 13-9.a, BCO 21-4, BCO 24-1, BCO 26-2 | in conflict; Adopted | Overture 23 from Gulf Coast Presbytery | [ga48_2021 p.463](../markdown/ga48_2021.md#ga48-p463) |
| II.Q | [Permitting church courts to meet by telecommunication](../inquiries/ga48_2021__ci17.md) | The CCB found that amending BCO 10 to authorize telecommunication meetings would create a conflict with existing BCO and RAO provisions, though the overture was adopted. | BCO 10, RAO 1-1 | creates a conflict; Adopted | Overture 26 from Philadelphia Metro West Presbytery | [ga48_2021 p.463](../markdown/ga48_2021.md#ga48-p463) |
| II.R | [Requiring public announcement of excommunication](../inquiries/ga48_2021__ci18.md) | The CCB determined that amending BCO 36 to mandate public excommunication would create a conflict with current BCO provisions, though the overture was adopted. | BCO 36-2, BCO 36-6 | would create a conflict; Adopted | Overture 27 from Platte Valley Presbytery | [ga48_2021 p.463](../markdown/ga48_2021.md#ga48-p463) |
| II.S | [Codifying sequence of steps in discipline investigations](../inquiries/ga48_2021__ci19.md) | The CCB found proposed amendments to BCO 31-2 prescribing an investigation sequence to be in conflict at multiple points with existing BCO provisions, though the overture was adopted. | BCO 31-2, BCO 32-19 | in conflict at multiple points; Adopted | Overture 28 from Philadelphia Metro West Presbytery | [ga48_2021 p.464](../markdown/ga48_2021.md#ga48-p464) |
| II.T | [Adding BCO appendix of investigation practice suggestions](../inquiries/ga48_2021__ci20.md) | The CCB found an overture adding a BCO appendix of investigation guidelines to be in conflict with the BCO, though the overture was adopted. | BCO 31-2 | in conflict; Adopted | Overture 29 from Philadelphia Metro West Presbytery | [ga48_2021 p.464](../markdown/ga48_2021.md#ga48-p464) |
| II.U | [Amending BCO to clarify moral requirements for church office](../inquiries/ga48_2021__ci21.md) | The CCB found that proposed amendments to BCO 21 and 24 clarifying moral qualifications for church office were not in conflict with the BCO, and the overture was adopted. | BCO 21, BCO 24, BCO 13-6, BCO 21-4 | not in conflict; Adopted | Overture 30 from Lowcountry Presbytery | [ga48_2021 p.464](../markdown/ga48_2021.md#ga48-p464) |
| II.V | [Process for converting assistant pastor to associate pastor](../inquiries/ga48_2021__ci22.md) | The CCB found that amending BCO 22 to clarify the process for an assistant pastor becoming an associate pastor was not in conflict with the BCO, and the overture was adopted. | BCO 22, BCO 20-2, BCO 22-1 | not in conflict; Adopted | Overture 31 from Northwest Georgia Presbytery | [ga48_2021 p.464](../markdown/ga48_2021.md#ga48-p464) |
| II.W | [Confession document requirements for cases without process](../inquiries/ga48_2021__ci23.md) | The CCB found that amending BCO 38-1 regarding confession documentation in cases without process was not in conflict with the BCO, and the overture was adopted. | BCO 38-1 | not in conflict; Adopted | Overture 33 from Pacific Northwest Presbytery | [ga48_2021 p.464](../markdown/ga48_2021.md#ga48-p464) |
| II.X | [Confession timing requirements in cases without process](../inquiries/ga48_2021__ci24.md) | The CCB found that amending BCO 38-1 regarding timing of confession in cases without process was not in conflict with the BCO, and the overture was adopted. | BCO 38-1, BCO 42-2 | not in conflict; Adopted | Overture 34 from Pacific Northwest Presbytery | [ga48_2021 p.464](../markdown/ga48_2021.md#ga48-p464) |
| II.Y | [Right to counsel in discipline cases without process](../inquiries/ga48_2021__ci25.md) | The CCB found that amending BCO 38-1 regarding counsel for accused persons in cases without process was not in conflict with the BCO, and the overture was adopted. | BCO 38-1 | not in conflict; Adopted | Overture 35 from Pacific Northwest Presbytery | [ga48_2021 p.465](../markdown/ga48_2021.md#ga48-p465) |
| II.Z | [Clarifying moral qualifications for ordained office holders](../inquiries/ga48_2021__ci26.md) | The CCB found that Eastern Pennsylvania Presbytery's overture amending BCO 21-4 and 24-1 to clarify moral requirements for church office was not in conflict with the BCO, and was adopted. | BCO 21-4, BCO 24-1, BCO 13-6 | not in conflict; Adopted | Overture 37 from Eastern Pennsylvania Presbytery | [ga48_2021 p.465](../markdown/ga48_2021.md#ga48-p465) |
